name: Home
title: Home
description: Entry page that lets users quickly begin key automotive service tasks such as location search, service browsing, coupon discovery, and accessing related tools, using dynamic content from assigned interfaces.

core_features: Top-of-page ZIP search form that allows users to enter a ZIP code and navigate to the locations listing page, using the ZIP as a URL parameter.
Homepage data loading using getHomeFeaturedContent, optionally personalized when a ZIP URL parameter is present, to display featured service categories, services, coupons, and nearby locations.
Global services navigation powered by getServiceCategories to expose all active service categories as navigation links to the services catalog page.
Featured service categories strip that highlights a subset of categories returned by getHomeFeaturedContent and allows users to jump into pre-filtered service browsing.

Featured services list that exposes commonly used or promoted services from getHomeFeaturedContent and lets users navigate to the services catalog with preselected service/category information via URL parameters.
Featured coupons area that shows active promotional offers from getHomeFeaturedContent and provides a path to the full coupons page.

Nearby locations panel (when ZIP-based data is available) using getHomeFeaturedContent.nearby_quick_locations to surface close-by service centers with basic info and shortcuts to continue to detailed location or booking flows on other pages.

Quick action shortcuts to core tasks: browse services, view coupons & deals, view maintenance plans, request a quote, purchase gift cards, and set up maintenance reminders, each implemented as navigation actions to the respective pages with URL parameters when needed.
Header cart icon navigation to the cart checkout page so users can review or complete existing selections regardless of how they arrived at the home page.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Start with a ZIP search and go to locations listing
1) User lands on Home (optionally with ?zip in the URL).
2) On page load, the home-data-loader component reads the zip URL parameter (if present) and calls getHomeFeaturedContent(zip) with it; it also calls getServiceCategories().
3) The home-zip-search-form component shows an empty or prefilled ZIP input (prefill uses the zip URL parameter if present).
4) User enters or edits the ZIP value and submits the form.
5) The onSubmitZipSearch handler validates that a non-empty ZIP value was entered.
6) If valid, the browser navigates to locations.html?zip=<enteredZip>, starting the dedicated locations flow.
7) If invalid, an inline validation error is shown in the ZIP form and navigation does not occur.

Workflow: Browse services from home
1) User lands on Home; home-data-loader calls getServiceCategories() and getHomeFeaturedContent(zipOrUndefined).
2) The services-category-nav component renders navigation links for each returned ServiceCategory.
3) The home-featured-service-categories component displays a subset defined by getHomeFeaturedContent.featured_service_categories.
4) User can click the general "Browse Services" quick action, triggering navigation to services.html.
5) Alternatively, user clicks a specific category link (either from the services-category-nav or the featured categories strip).
6) The click handler builds a URL such as services.html?category=<categorySlug> and optionally appends &zip=<currentZipFromUrlParamIfAny>.
7) Browser navigates to that URL, where the services page will use the parameters to filter services (out of scope for this page).

Workflow: Use featured services to jump into detailed service browsing
1) User lands on Home; home-data-loader has already loaded data from getHomeFeaturedContent.
2) The home-featured-services-list component receives featured_services and renders each service with its name, category_name, category_slug, and starting_price_from.
3) User clicks on a featured service item.
4) The onClickFeaturedService handler reads the clicked item's service_id and category_slug.
5) The handler constructs a URL such as services.html?serviceId=<service_id>&category=<category_slug> and, if a zip URL parameter exists on Home, appends &zip=<zip>.
6) Browser navigates to that services.html URL so the user can see more details or start booking for that specific service.

Workflow: Check current deals from the homepage
1) User lands on Home; getHomeFeaturedContent(...) is invoked and returns featured_coupons.
2) The home-featured-coupons-list component renders a set of coupon summaries based on the returned data.
3) User scans the list and, if desired, clicks on a coupon item or a "View all coupons" action.
4) Any coupon or "View all" click triggers navigation to coupons.html (optionally including &zip=<currentZipIfPresent> so downstream pages can localize offers).
5) The coupons page (not part of this design) can then load full coupon details based on its own logic.

Workflow: Quickly navigate to maintenance plans, quote requests, gift cards, and reminders
1) User lands on Home; home-data-loader completes its data loading tasks.
2) The home-quick-actions component displays quick links to: services.html, coupons.html, maintenance_plans.html, quote_request.html.
3) The home-secondary-tiles component displays navigation tiles or links for gift_cards.html and maintenance_reminders.html.
4) User clicks any of these actions.
5) The corresponding click handler constructs the destination URL, optionally attaching &zip=<currentZipIfPresent> when relevant (for pages that may localize content) and navigates the browser to that page.

Workflow: Use nearby locations quick panel to move toward booking
1) User arrives on Home with a zip URL parameter or via another path where a ZIP is available; home-data-loader calls getHomeFeaturedContent(zip).
2) getHomeFeaturedContent returns nearby_quick_locations, which is passed into the home-nearby-locations-panel component.
3) The component renders a list of nearby locations, including distance_miles, average_rating, rating_count, open/closed status, and any available oil change price or next available slot information.
4) User clicks on a location item or on a specific action (such as a "view location" link in that item).
5) The click handler constructs locations.html?locationId=<location_id> and, if there is a current ZIP on the Home URL, may also include zip=<zip>.
6) Browser navigates to the locations page, which can use these parameters to highlight or default to that location.

Workflow: Access cart from Home
1) User lands on Home from any entry path.
2) The header-cart-icon component is shown as part of the global header.
3) User clicks the cart icon.
4) The onClickCartIcon handler navigates the browser to cart_checkout.html.
5) The cart and checkout logic for that page are handled there (outside the scope of the Home page).
interactions: ZIP input change: When the user types into the ZIP input field in the home-zip-search-form component, onChangeZip updates the component's internal state for the current ZIP value.
ZIP form submit: When the user submits the ZIP form (via button click or Enter key), onSubmitZipSearch validates the ZIP field and, if valid, navigates the browser to locations.html?zip=<enteredZip>.

Browse services quick action: Clicking the "Browse Services" quick action in home-quick-actions triggers onClickBrowseServices, which navigates to services.html, optionally appending &zip=<currentZipFromHomeUrlIfAny>.
Quick action to coupons: Clicking the "View Coupons & Deals" quick action triggers onClickViewCoupons, which navigates to coupons.html (optionally preserving a current ZIP via URL parameter).
Quick action to maintenance plans: Clicking the "Maintenance Plans" quick action triggers onClickMaintenancePlans, navigating to maintenance_plans.html with optional ZIP parameter propagation.
Quick action to quote request: Clicking the "Request a Quote" quick action triggers onClickRequestQuote, navigating to quote_request.html with optional ZIP URL parameter propagation.
Gift cards tile click: Clicking the gift cards tile in home-secondary-tiles triggers onClickGiftCards to navigate to gift_cards.html.
Maintenance reminders tile click: Clicking the maintenance reminders tile triggers onClickMaintenanceReminders to navigate to maintenance_reminders.html.

Service category nav click: In services-category-nav, clicking a category link triggers onClickCategoryLink, which navigates to services.html?category=<categorySlug> and may append &zip=<currentZipIfPresent>.

Featured service category click: In home-featured-service-categories, clicking a featured category triggers onClickFeaturedCategory, which navigates to services.html?category=<categorySlug>&source=home_featured, with optional ZIP propagation.

Featured service item click: In home-featured-services-list, clicking a service triggers onClickFeaturedService, which builds a URL such as services.html?serviceId=<service_id>&category=<category_slug>&source=home_featured and navigates there.

Featured coupon click: In home-featured-coupons-list, clicking a coupon card triggers onClickCouponCard, which navigates the user to coupons.html (optionally including identifiers such as couponId=<coupon_id> and the current ZIP).
View all coupons click: In home-featured-coupons-list, clicking a "View all coupons" action triggers onClickViewAllCoupons to navigate to coupons.html, possibly with a &zip parameter.
Nearby location row click: In home-nearby-locations-panel, clicking a location row triggers onClickLocationRow, which navigates to locations.html?locationId=<location_id>&zip=<currentZipIfPresent>.

Nearby location booking-related action click: In home-nearby-locations-panel, clicking a booking-oriented action for a location (e.g., related to oil changes) triggers onClickBookOilChange, which navigates to services.html?category=oil_changes&locationId=<location_id>&zip=<currentZipIfPresent>.
Header cart icon click: Clicking the header cart icon triggers onClickCartIcon, navigating to cart_checkout.html.

Initial page load: When Home loads, home-data-loader handles onInit, reads URL parameters (such as zip), invokes getHomeFeaturedContent(zipOrUndefined) and getServiceCategories(), and then distributes the resulting data to children components.

URL change on Home (soft navigation): If the application supports internal navigation that changes Home's URL parameters without a full reload, home-data-loader listens for onUrlChange and re-invokes getHomeFeaturedContent with the new zip parameter as needed.
page_functionality.state_logic: The Home page uses only URL parameters (and in-memory component state) for behavior; it does not rely on localStorage or other persistent client-side storage. The primary URL parameter for this page is zip. On initial load or when the URL changes, home-data-loader reads the zip parameter from window.location. If zip is present and non-empty, it passes that value into getHomeFeaturedContent(zip). If zip is absent, undefined, or empty, getHomeFeaturedContent is invoked without a ZIP value so the backend can decide what default content to return. The response from getHomeFeaturedContent determines the contents of featured_service_categories, featured_services, featured_coupons, and nearby_quick_locations. The services-category-nav component always uses the full set from getServiceCategories(), independent of URL parameters. When Home triggers outgoing navigations (to locations.html, services.html, coupons.html, maintenance_plans.html, quote_request.html, gift_cards.html, maintenance_reminders.html, or cart_checkout.html), it encodes relevant context in the target URL's query string. Common context includes the user's ZIP (if available from Home's zip parameter) and any selected categorySlug, serviceId, locationId, or couponId, so downstream pages can filter or preselect content. Any UI-level selections that are only relevant within Home (such as which featured item is expanded, or transient validation states for the ZIP form) are stored only in component-local state and are not persisted in the URL or any external storage.

components.functionality: Coordinates all data fetching for the Home page. On initialization and whenever Home's URL changes, it reads the zip query parameter, calls getHomeFeaturedContent with that ZIP (or without it if not provided), calls getServiceCategories, normalizes the returned data structures, and passes data into child components as props or via a shared state container.

Provides a single input for the user to enter a ZIP code and submit to view nearby locations. It optionally pre-populates the input value with the zip URL parameter from Home. Submission navigates the user to the locations listing page with the entered ZIP encoded in the URL.

Displays a set of prominent links for primary actions: browse services, view coupons & deals, view maintenance plans, and request a quote. Each action is implemented as a navigation event to the appropriate page, preserving the current ZIP parameter in the destination URL when available.

Shows a navigation menu of all active service categories returned by getServiceCategories, typically in the header or a primary navigation area. Clicking a category navigates to services.html with that category's slug encoded as a URL parameter so the services page can filter by category.

Consumes featured_service_categories from getHomeFeaturedContent and displays them as quick-access category entries on Home. Each entry links to the services catalog, passing the category slug (and optional ZIP) as URL parameters to help pre-filter the services list.

Displays the featured_services array from getHomeFeaturedContent, including service name, category, and starting price information. When a user selects a service, the component builds a services.html URL containing serviceId, category, and optional zip parameters to guide the user into that specific service's detailed flow.

Renders featured_coupons from getHomeFeaturedContent as a list of coupon summaries showing the name, discount label, category, short description, and any expiration metadata. Clicking a coupon (or a global "view all" action) navigates the user to coupons.html, with optional couponId and zip parameters included in the URL.

Uses nearby_quick_locations from getHomeFeaturedContent to display a list of nearby automotive service locations, including basic details like city, state, zip, distance, ratings, open/closed status, and any oil change pricing/availability data. Each location row offers actions that navigate to the locations page (for location details) or to the services page (for starting a location-specific service flow), passing locationId, and optionally zip, as URL parameters.

Provides secondary call-to-action tiles or links for gift card purchases and maintenance reminder setup. Each tile triggers navigation to the respective page (gift_cards.html or maintenance_reminders.html), optionally retaining any existing zip parameter in the URL for future personalization.

A header-level component that exposes the shopping cart entry point from the Home page. It does not load or modify cart data itself; instead, when clicked, it navigates the user to cart_checkout.html where cart operations are handled.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a top-to-bottom, stacked layout with a slim utility/header zone, then a strong photographic hero, then an overlapping two-column interaction zone, followed by more conventional stacked two-column content bands. The key task (find services near me) is placed in the left side of the first overlapping section, mirroring the mockup’s left-aligned service text block. Supporting discovery elements (featured services, coupons, tools) are arranged beneath in clearly separated sections that still feel like one continuous scroll.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a top-to-bottom, stacked layout with a slim utility/header zone, then a strong photographic hero, then an overlapping two-column interaction zone, followed by more conventional stacked two-column content bands. The key task (find services near me) is placed in the left side of the first overlapping section, mirroring the mockup’s left-aligned service text block. Supporting discovery elements (featured services, coupons, tools) are arranged beneath in clearly separated sections that still feel like one continuous scroll.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport is reserved for the utility bar and main header (where the cart icon lives). The hero image spans the full width and occupies about 25–35% of the initial viewport height. The overlapping search-and-locations band takes up the remaining part of the first screen: a two-column 7/5 split with the left column for search and quick actions and the right column for nearby locations. Below this, featured categories, services, coupons, and secondary tiles are stacked in bands with 32–48px vertical separation and generous side margins (~80–120px at large viewports) within a centered max-width container.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate: each functional block (search, quick actions, lists) has clear padding (16–24px) and line spacing, avoiding clutter. Lists (services, coupons, locations) show a handful of items above the fold with comfortable spacing between cards. Horizontal strips like the category nav and featured category strip are visually compact but not cramped, reflecting the brochure-like, practical tone of the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F-pattern: attention starts at the logo/header (implied) and header cart icon area, scans across navigation, then drops to the left-aligned hero heading. From there, the eye moves down into the left-side overlapping panel where the ZIP search form and quick actions sit as the primary interaction. After processing these, the eye is pulled right to the nearby locations panel, then continues down to the horizontal services category nav, the featured categories strip, and finally into the lower two-column services/coupons section and bottom secondary tiles. Blue and yellow brand accents and photography subtly support a Z-pattern in the top region on first load but reading behavior stabilizes into left-to-right, top-to-bottom scanning.

layout.overall_description: The home page sits inside a centered, max-width 12-column grid framed by generous left and right margins. A slim yellow utility bar and white header with logo and navigation span the full width at the very top; the cart icon anchors the far right of this bar, echoing the mockup’s right-aligned social icon. Immediately below, a full-width hero photograph of the workshop or storefront fills roughly the upper third of the screen, with a bold white heading on the left side announcing the home experience (e.g., ‘Auto Services Near You’). Along the lower edge of this hero, a horizontal services category nav runs across the center, acting as a bridge into the main content. The first main interaction zone overlaps the bottom of the hero: on the left, a light, semi-transparent panel floats above the hero background, housing the ZIP search form at the top and a row/grid of quick action shortcuts beneath. On the right, aligned to the page’s right edge, a solid panel shows nearby locations as stacked cards; this visually replaces the mechanic photo from the mockup as the right-side focal area. Below this overlapping band, the layout transitions into stacked content sections: a horizontal strip of featured service categories, then a two-column section with featured services on the left and coupons on the right, all in light, flat cards with comfortable padding. At the bottom, secondary navigation tiles spread across the width in a simple grid, offering access to tools and less critical journeys. The result feels like a clear, trustworthy brochure site adapted to modern task flows, with the strongest functional elements anchored in that first overlapping two-column band under the hero.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader component is non-visual and conceptually wraps the main content area starting just below the header/hero region. It spans the full width of the central content container (matching the 12-column grid) and sits underneath the hero section and above all interactive blocks (ZIP search, quick actions, lists). When loading states are needed, it can present subtle skeleton placeholders that cover the overlapping search-and-locations band and the first set of lists, but it does not occupy dedicated visible space once content has loaded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a full-width, flow-based wrapper within the main content stack, sitting directly under the hero section. It can temporarily overlay children with loading states but otherwise collapses to no intrinsic height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The ZIP search form is positioned in the top portion of the left-hand overlapping panel that sits immediately below and partially overlapping the hero’s bottom edge. This panel occupies roughly 7 of 12 grid columns, aligned to the left margin of the content container, and floats over the lower-left area of the hero image by about 48–64px. Inside this panel, the search form is anchored at the top, spanning about 80–90% of the panel width with 24px padding on all sides. It consists of a single prominent input and a primary button, sized to stand out as the main action above the fold. The panel’s background is a light, slightly translucent white or very light grey, echoing the semi-transparent text block in the mockup, clearly separating the form from the underlying photography while still letting a hint of the hero image show through.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the flow of the overlapping left column, but visually appears to float over the hero due to negative top margin / overlap. It maintains its left alignment and approximate 7/12 width down to tablet sizes, stacking above quick actions when vertical space is constrained.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quick actions component sits directly below the ZIP search form within the same left overlapping panel. It uses the remaining vertical space of this panel, with a comfortable 16–24px gap under the search form. Quick actions are arranged as a horizontal row of buttons or a 2×2 grid of tiles (depending on available width), spanning the same 80–90% width as the search form and centered within the panel, while still aligned flush to the left grid column. Each quick action tile has 16px internal padding and 16px gaps between items, reflecting the moderate content density. This cluster forms a clear second focal area after the search box, all within the single unified card-like block on the left that visually mirrors the logbook servicing text block from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and bound to the left overlapping panel. On smaller viewports, quick actions wrap to multiple rows but remain directly under the search form, preserving their tight grouping as a single interaction zone.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The services category nav is positioned at the bottom of the hero section, acting as a horizontal menu that visually bridges the hero and the overlapping content below. It spans roughly 8 of 12 grid columns and is centered horizontally within the main content width, sitting 16–24px above the hero’s bottom edge. Each navigation item is a simple text link or pill, arranged in a single row with even spacing, following the mockup’s straightforward horizontal navigation style. When the overlapping left panel rises into the hero space, this nav appears just above and slightly behind it in the vertical stack, enhancing the layered effect while remaining clearly readable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero section. It scrolls with the page and does not stick, but is visually anchored to the hero’s bottom boundary. On smaller screens, items may wrap to two lines while still centered under the hero heading.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured service categories strip is placed directly below the overlapping search-and-locations band, separated by approximately 32–40px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the central content container (all 12 columns) but uses internal padding of 24px and a subtle background block (light grey or very pale blue) to read as its own section. Within this strip, category tiles are laid out horizontally in a row (or a gently wrapping grid), each tile occupying 2–3 columns at desktop widths. The strip visually echoes the hero’s horizontal category nav but provides more visual weight and click targets, acting as a scannable summary of major service groups.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width section within the main content column. On narrower viewports, tiles reflow into multiple rows while the overall section remains a clearly separated band.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured services list appears in a lower main content section that uses a balanced two-column layout. This section sits beneath the featured service categories strip with 32–48px of vertical separation. Within this band, the featured services list occupies the left half of the content area, spanning roughly 6 of 12 columns. It is contained within a simple card-like block with a white background, a subtle border or shadow, and 24px internal padding. The list presents a vertical stack of service items (3–6 above the fold), each separated by 16px gaps, maintaining the mockup’s readable, brochure-style density. A heading at the top of the block aligns left, echoing the left-aligned typography used in the mockup’s text sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within a two-column section. On tablet and smaller screens, this block stacks above the coupons list, stretching to full width while retaining its left alignment and internal spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured coupons list is placed to the right of the featured services list within the same two-column section. It occupies the right 6 of 12 columns, aligned to the right margin of the content container. Like the services list, it sits inside a white, padded block, but with stronger yellow accent elements (badges, banners, or price highlights) to visually differentiate coupons. Items are arranged in a vertical list (3–5 above the fold), with 16px spacing and a modest hierarchy: coupon title, savings highlight, and a short description. The overall block height roughly matches or slightly undercuts the left services list, keeping the horizontal band visually balanced.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within a two-column layout. On smaller screens, it stacks directly under the featured services list, maintaining a clear vertical separation with 24–32px margin.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The nearby locations panel occupies the right side of the overlapping hero-adjacent section, mirroring the mechanic photo placement in the mockup. It spans roughly 5 of 12 grid columns and is aligned flush with the right edge of the main content container. Vertically, its top edge aligns approximately with the top of the left overlapping panel (search and quick actions), so both panels feel like a combined two-column unit overlaying the lower part of the hero. The panel has a solid white or very light background with 24px padding and displays 2–4 location cards stacked vertically, each with a modest amount of spacing and clear typography. This positions the locations as a strong secondary focal area: users first see the main search on the left, then glance right to see actual nearby options.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static but visually overlapping the hero background through a negative top margin. On medium screens, it remains in the right column; on narrow screens, it stacks below the left panel while preserving its role as a distinct card-like section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The secondary tiles component is placed near the bottom of the page, below the featured services and coupons section, separated by a clear 32–48px vertical gap. It spans the full width of the main content container and presents a grid of smaller navigation tiles (for tools, account, resources, etc.). Tiles are arranged in 3 or 4 columns per row at desktop widths, each occupying 3–4 of 12 columns with 16–24px gaps between them. Internal padding is moderate (16px per tile), and visual styling is simple: flat colored or lightly outlined blocks with an icon and label. This grid provides additional navigation options without competing with the primary search and location blocks, mirroring the mockup’s practical, no-frills card style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static full-width grid. On smaller screens, tiles stack into 2 columns or a single column while maintaining consistent gaps and section padding.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header cart icon sits in the main header bar at the very top of the page, directly reflecting the mockup’s right-aligned social icon. It is positioned on the far right of the horizontal header area, vertically centered within a 64–80px-tall white header bar. The logo is assumed on the left and primary navigation items centered; the cart icon is isolated on the right with sufficient horizontal padding (24–32px from the right edge) to remain visually distinct. Its size is modest but clearly legible, matching the small-business corporate style rather than oversized, trendy iconography.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contained within a (likely) sticky header that stays fixed to the top of the viewport as the user scrolls, maintaining constant visibility. The icon itself is aligned inline with other header elements and does not float independently.

name: Locations Search & Results
title: Locations Search & Results
description: Search for automotive service locations by ZIP or city, refine results with filters, and navigate to location details or booking.

core_features: Accept a ZIP code or city input and call the searchLocations interface to retrieve matching service locations.
Optionally, when the user wants only locations with a free shuttle within a minimum radius, call searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter using the ZIP and minimum radius filter.

Display search results as a list of locations including distance, rating, rating count, open/closed status for today, shuttle availability, and starting prices per service category from searchLocations results.
Allow users to filter results by minimum rating, open-now status, late hours (e.g., open past a configurable time), free shuttle, and other amenity-related filters using searchLocations filters.
Sort results client-side by distance (default) and optionally by other sortable fields returned from searchLocations such as rating or name.
Allow users to click a location card to navigate to location_detail.html with the corresponding locationId in the URL.
Allow users to click a "Book at this Location" action on a result to navigate to schedule_service.html with the corresponding locationId in the URL.
Keep current search term, filters, and sort state encoded in URL parameters so the page can be reloaded, bookmarked, or navigated via browser history while preserving state.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Initial page load with optional ZIP from homepage:
1) User navigates to locations.html, optionally with a zip parameter in the URL.
2) The page reads the zip parameter and other search-related URL parameters (if present) and initializes the search form and filter controls.
3) If a zip parameter or another search term is present, the page composes a filters object from URL parameters and calls searchLocations with zipOrCity and filters.
4) The page shows a loading state, then displays the resulting list sorted by distance.
5) The user can immediately click on a location card or the "Book at this Location" button to navigate to the respective pages with locationId in the URL.

Workflow 2 – User performs a new search by ZIP or city:
1) User enters a ZIP code or city in the search input.
2) User submits the search form.
3) The page updates the URL parameters to reflect the new search term and resets or preserves filter parameters according to current UI state.
4) The page calls searchLocations with the entered zipOrCity and the current filters.
5) Results are displayed and sorted by distance; the status bar shows how many matching locations were found.
6) User may refine further with filters or navigate to a location detail/booking page from any result.

Workflow 3 – User filters for free shuttle and late hours:
1) After search results are displayed, the user enables the free shuttle filter.
2) If the user has a ZIP in the URL and enables a specialized shuttle-only mode, the page optionally calls searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter with zip and a configurable minimum shuttle radius.
3) Otherwise, the page updates filters and re-calls searchLocations with offers_free_shuttle reflected in the filters object.
4) The URL parameters are updated to include shuttle-related flags and any minimum shuttle radius value.
5) The user optionally also enables a late-hours filter (e.g., open past a specific time), which changes the filters and triggers another call to searchLocations.
6) The results list updates to show only locations matching both free shuttle and late-hours criteria.

Workflow 4 – Navigating to location details or booking:
1) User finds a suitable location in the result list.
2) User clicks on the location card body to view more information.
3) The page navigates to location_detail.html with locationId set to the selected location_id from the search result item.
4) Alternatively, the user clicks "Book at this Location" on the same card.
5) The page navigates to schedule_service.html with locationId set to the same location_id, where the booking flow continues.
interactions: Search input interaction: User types ZIP or city into the search field; on form submit, the page prevents default submission, updates URL parameters (including the main search term), and invokes searchLocations with zipOrCity and the current filter set.

Filter change interaction: When the user adjusts any filter (minimum rating, open-now toggle, late-hours toggle, free shuttle toggle, or other amenity toggles), the page immediately updates the corresponding URL parameters, recomputes the filters object, and calls searchLocations again (or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter when in shuttle-only mode with ZIP).

Shuttle-only shortcut interaction: When the user enables a shuttle-only mode (for example, a dedicated "Show locations with free shuttle" toggle), the page checks that a ZIP is available from the search input or URL; if available, it calls searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter with zip and minShuttleRadiusMiles, updates URL parameters to indicate shuttle-only mode, and refreshes the results list with returned shuttle-capable locations.

Sort interaction: When the user changes the sort option (e.g., from distance to rating), the page updates a sort parameter in the URL and re-sorts the already-fetched result set client-side without recalling the search interfaces. If needed, new searches after changing filters keep this sort preference in effect.

Result selection interaction – view details: When the user clicks a location card container, the page reads the location_id from that result and navigates to location_detail.html?locationId=<location_id>. No additional data fetching occurs on this page for that navigation.

Result selection interaction – book now: When the user clicks the "Book at this Location" action on a result card, the page reads the location_id and navigates to schedule_service.html?locationId=<location_id>, handing off control to the booking page.

Empty state interaction: If a search or filter combination returns an empty array, the page displays an empty-state component with guidance to adjust search term or filters; interacting with any of these suggestions updates filters and/or search term, updates URL parameters, and re-invokes the appropriate search interface.

Error and retry interaction: If any call to searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter fails, the page shows an error message and a retry control; clicking retry reuses the current search term and filters from URL state to call the same interface again.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses URL parameters as the single source of truth for search and filter state. The primary URL parameters include: zipOrCity (search term; if only zip is provided from index.html it is mapped into this), minRating (minimum allowed average_rating), openNow (boolean flag indicating that only locations with is_open_now=true should be included), openLate (boolean or a time string defining a late closing threshold, used to build appropriate filters for searchLocations), shuttleOnly (boolean flag indicating free-shuttle-only mode), minShuttleRadiusMiles (numeric value passed to searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter when shuttleOnly is true and a ZIP is present), sortBy (sorting key such as distance, rating, or name), and any additional amenity flags. On initial load, the page reads these parameters to initialize UI controls and constructs the filters object used for searchLocations. If shuttleOnly is true and zip is present, the page loads using searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter; otherwise it uses searchLocations. Any user action that changes search term, filters, or sort updates the URL parameters via pushState or replaceState and then either re-invokes the relevant search interface or re-sorts the existing results. Browser back/forward navigation is handled by listening to URL changes (popstate) and re-synchronizing the UI and search calls according to the parameters without relying on localStorage or other persistent local state.

components.functionality: Accepts a ZIP code or city input from the user, initializes its value from the zip or search-related URL parameters, and triggers location searches using searchLocations (or, when appropriate, initializes shuttle-only mode). It composes the zipOrCity argument for the search interface and collaborates with filter and state-manager components to apply current filters.
data_binding: URL parameter: zip (from index.html) mapped into the search term
URL parameter: general search term (e.g., zipOrCity)
event_handlers: onInit: Read zip and other search-related URL parameters and set the input value accordingly.
onInputChange: Update internal search term state but do not immediately invoke search.

onSubmit: Prevent default form submission, update URL search term parameters, reset pagination if implemented, and call searchLocations with zipOrCity set to the current input and filters obtained from the filter-controls component.
onExternalStateSync: When URL parameters change via back/forward navigation, update the input field to reflect the current search term.

components.functionality: Manages the set of filters for the locations search, including minimum rating, open-now, open-late, free shuttle, and any other amenity flags. It reads initial values from URL parameters, updates them when the user interacts, and triggers new calls to searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter as appropriate.
data_binding: Location fields exposed via searchLocations: average_rating, is_open_now, today_close_time, offers_free_shuttle
URL parameters: minRating, openNow, openLate, shuttleOnly, minShuttleRadiusMiles, and any amenity-related flags
event_handlers: onInit: Parse URL parameters and set the initial state of rating sliders, switches, and amenity toggles.
onFilterChange: Whenever a filter control changes, update the corresponding URL parameter and notify the search-state-manager to re-run the search.

onShuttleFilterToggle: When the shuttle-only toggle changes, set shuttleOnly in URL parameters and coordinate with the shuttle-search-switcher component to choose between searchLocations and searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter.
onClearAllFilters: Reset all filter controls to their default state, clear related URL parameters, and trigger a fresh search with minimal filters.

components.functionality: Controls the logic for free-shuttle-focused searches. When shuttle-only mode is enabled and a ZIP is available, it calls searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter using the ZIP and a configurable minimum shuttle radius. When disabled, it uses searchLocations with filters.offers_free_shuttle set appropriately.
data_binding: searchLocations interface parameter: filters.offers_free_shuttle and other filters
event_handlers: onInit: Check URL for shuttleOnly and minShuttleRadiusMiles; if shuttleOnly is true and zip is available, trigger an initial search using searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter.

onToggleOn: When user enables shuttle-only mode, verify that a ZIP is present (from the search form or URL). If present, write shuttleOnly and minShuttleRadiusMiles to URL parameters and call searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter; if not present, prompt the user via the search-form to provide a ZIP.
onToggleOff: When user disables shuttle-only mode, clear shuttleOnly-specific URL parameters as needed and trigger searchLocations with filters.offers_free_shuttle reflecting the new state.
onRadiusChange: When user changes the shuttle radius threshold, update minShuttleRadiusMiles in URL parameters and re-call searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter if shuttle-only mode is active.

components.functionality: Allows users to change the sort order of the currently loaded search results (e.g., by distance, rating, or location name). Sorting is applied client-side to the array returned by searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter, without additional API calls.
event_handlers: onInit: Read sortBy from URL parameters and set the active sort option.
onSortChange: When a user selects a different sort option, update sortBy in URL parameters and re-sort the in-memory result list accordingly.
onExternalStateSync: When URL changes due to navigation, re-apply sorting based on the updated sortBy value.

components.functionality: Renders the list of location search results returned by either searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter. Delegates to location-result-card components for individual items and responds to sorting and filtering changes by re-rendering.
event_handlers: onResultsUpdate: When new data is returned from a search interface, update the list and render corresponding location-result-card instances.
onSortApplied: When sort-control indicates a new sort order, reorder the rendered list accordingly.
onEmptyResults: If the results array is empty, hide list content and show the empty-state component instead.

components.functionality: Displays a single location result with key details: name, address, city, state, ZIP, distance, average rating and rating count, today’s open/close times, is_open_now status, free shuttle availability and radius, and starting prices per service category. Provides actions to view detailed information or book at that location.
data_binding: searchLocations single result item: location_id, name, address_line1, city, state, zip, distance_miles, average_rating, rating_count, offers_free_shuttle, shuttle_radius_miles, today_open_time, today_close_time, is_open_now, starting_prices
searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter single result item: location_id, name, distance_miles, offers_free_shuttle, shuttle_radius_miles
event_handlers: onCardClick: Navigate to location_detail.html?locationId=<location_id> when the user clicks the main card body.
onBookClick: Navigate to schedule_service.html?locationId=<location_id> when the user clicks the "Book at this Location" action.
onKeyboardActivate: Support keyboard activation (e.g., Enter key) to trigger the same navigation as onCardClick for accessibility.

components.functionality: Summarizes the current search context and outcomes: shows the number of locations found, the active search term (ZIP or city), and key active filters. Provides a control to clear filters quickly.
data_binding: Current filters from filter-controls (minRating, openNow, openLate, shuttleOnly, amenities)
URL parameters corresponding to filters and sortBy
event_handlers: onResultsUpdate: Update displayed count whenever a new search result array is received.
onFilterStateChange: Update the list of visible badges or labels that represent active filters.
onClearFiltersClick: Invoke filter-controls.onClearAllFilters to reset filters and trigger a new search.

components.functionality: Displays a message and simple controls when no locations are returned by the current search and filters. Offers quick actions like clearing filters or adjusting search parameters.
data_binding: Current filters and search term (for contextual messaging, without hardcoding any data values)
URL parameters representing filters and search term
event_handlers: onShow: Triggered when results length is zero; hides the locations-results-list and shows the empty-state.
onClearFiltersClick: Calls filter-controls.onClearAllFilters to remove filters and triggers a new search.
onAdjustSearchClick: Focuses the location-search-form input so the user can easily modify the ZIP or city.

components.functionality: Tracks the status of calls to searchLocations and searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter. Shows a loading indicator while requests are in progress and an error message with retry functionality if requests fail.
data_binding: Last used parameters: zipOrCity, filters, zip, minShuttleRadiusMiles (for retry purposes)
event_handlers: onSearchStart: When any search interface is invoked, set the state to loading and notify the UI to display a loading indicator.
onSearchSuccess: When results are returned, clear loading state and hide any previous error messages.
onSearchError: When a request fails, store error state and show an error message with a retry option.
onRetryClick: Reinvoke the last-used search interface (searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter) with the same parameters.

components.functionality: Coordinates all URL parameter parsing and updates, ensuring that search term, filters, and sort state are consistently mirrored between the UI and the URL. Handles browser navigation events so that the page can restore the correct state after back/forward navigation.
data_binding: URL parameters: zip, zipOrCity, minRating, openNow, openLate, shuttleOnly, minShuttleRadiusMiles, sortBy, and any amenity flags
Internal state of location-search-form, filter-controls, and results-sort-control
event_handlers: onInit: Parse initial URL parameters and distribute state to location-search-form, filter-controls, and results-sort-control. Decide whether to trigger searchLocations or searchLocationsWithShuttleFilter based on shuttleOnly and zip.

onStateChangeFromUI: When any UI component requests a URL change (e.g., new search term, filters, sort), build the updated query string and call history.pushState or history.replaceState to update the URL without full reload.

onPopState: When the user navigates via browser back/forward, re-parse the URL parameters and notify all dependent components to update their state and, if necessary, re-trigger the appropriate search interface.
onReset: Optionally clear all search-related URL parameters when a full reset is requested by the user.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-stacked structure as the mockup: global utility bar and header, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/branding image and a bold left-aligned page title like “Find a Service Location.” Directly overlapping the lower edge of the hero, a primary two-column content section holds the search and filters on the left and the results list on the right. All search-related controls are grouped into a single left-side panel that partially overlays the hero image, mirroring the original ‘text block over image’ pattern, while the right side presents the scrollable results cards.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-stacked structure as the mockup: global utility bar and header, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/branding image and a bold left-aligned page title like “Find a Service Location.” Directly overlapping the lower edge of the hero, a primary two-column content section holds the search and filters on the left and the results list on the right. All search-related controls are grouped into a single left-side panel that partially overlays the hero image, mirroring the original ‘text block over image’ pattern, while the right side presents the scrollable results cards.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Header+utility occupy ~10–15% of the viewport height; the hero banner uses another ~25–30%. The overlapping two-column search/results section fills the remaining initial viewport, with the left panel (search form + filters + key toggles) taking about 40–45% of the content width and the right column (status, sort, results list) about 55–60%. Content is centered within a max-width container, leaving generous left/right margins (approximately 80–120px at large desktop) and consistent vertical section spacing of 32–48px between major bands.
layout_strategies.content_density: Density is moderate and brochure-like: the search form, filters, and sort controls are clearly separated with 16–24px padding and vertical gaps. Blocks use flat background color panels with readable line heights and sufficient whitespace. The results column contains stacked cards with visible breathing room between them, avoiding cramped tabular layouts. Filter options and toggles are grouped logically, but never packed tightly; key actions like booking links in cards remain immediately discoverable.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow preserves an F-pattern. The eye starts at the logo and top navigation, then moves right across the yellow utility bar and nav. It drops to the hero’s left-aligned page title, then continues straight down into the left search panel (location search form first, then shuttle toggle and filters). After scanning those controls, attention shifts horizontally to the top of the results column where the status bar and sort control sit, and then flows downward through the stack of location cards. The strong left column (search/filter) anchors decision-making, while the right column provides the main scannable content, encouraging a left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading rhythm.

layout.overall_description: Below the familiar yellow utility bar and white header, a full-width hero image of the workshop or storefront stretches edge to edge, with a bold white heading like “Find a Service Location” positioned in the left-middle of the image. Overlapping the bottom of this hero, a light, semi-transparent panel appears, aligned to the left half of the page: this panel holds the location search form at the top, followed by shuttle and filter controls, creating a compact command center for refining results. To the right, starting a little lower so the overlap is visually clear, the results column runs from just under the hero down the page: at its top, a slim status bar and sort control share a horizontal row, and beneath them a vertical list of location cards fills the space, each card spanning the width of the right column. The two-column layout sits within a centered max-width container, leaving generous side gutters and using consistent vertical spacing, so the page feels structured and trustworthy. As users scroll, the header and hero move out of view, while the left panel and right results list scroll together, maintaining a clear split between ‘refine’ on the left and ‘browse’ on the right, with empty, loading, and error states occupying the same right-column area where the cards normally appear.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location search form is the anchor of the overlapping left panel directly beneath the hero. It sits in the upper portion of a light-colored or semi-transparent rectangular block that starts roughly one-third up from the bottom of the hero image and extends downward into the main content area, mimicking the overlapping text panel from the mockup. Horizontally, this panel occupies about 40–45% of the page’s content width, aligned flush with the left edge of the max-width container and inset from the viewport edges with generous side margins. The search form itself spans nearly the full width of this panel, leaving 16–24px padding on all sides. The primary input (ZIP/city) is centered within the panel horizontally but left-aligned for text, with label above and the main submit button to the right of or below the input, depending on available width. Vertical spacing above the form (from the hero’s bottom edge) is about 24–32px, giving it breathing room while keeping it clearly associated with the page title in the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow; it scrolls with the page along with the left panel. The panel itself overlaps the hero image visually but is not fixed or sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter controls are placed directly beneath the location search form within the same left-side overlapping panel, forming a vertical ‘sidebar’ of refinement options. They inherit the width of the panel (about 40–45% of the content width) and are separated from the search form by a clear vertical gap of 16–24px. Filters are stacked in discrete groups (e.g., services offered, distance range) with group headings left-aligned and individual controls arranged in one or two columns depending on label length, always preserving comfortable padding (16px) inside the panel edges. The bottom of the filter region ends slightly above the bottom edge of the right-hand results column’s first visible cards, maintaining visual balance. On narrower viewports (tablet/mobile), these controls shift to full width under the search form, remaining grouped as a block but losing the side-by-side appearance with the results column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and scrolling as part of the left column. On large screens it behaves like a sidebar within the overlapping panel; on small screens it becomes a stacked block under the search form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The shuttle search switcher is a compact toggle control placed near the top-right of the left panel, visually tied to the location search form but distinct from the bulk filter section. Within the panel, it sits on the same horizontal line as a subtle label like “Show locations with shuttle service,” aligned to the right edge of the panel content area. There is a small horizontal gap (8–16px) between the search input/button cluster and this switcher, so on large screens it feels like an inline, at-a-glance refinement option. Vertically, it appears just below or level with the main search input, separated by 8–12px from any labels, ensuring it doesn’t crowd the primary field. When the layout stacks on smaller screens, the switcher shifts to sit directly under the search form, left-aligned with the form label, maintaining a clear association while preserving tap-friendly spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static; part of the left panel’s content. It reflows responsively from right-aligned inline placement on desktop to stacked placement under the search form on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results sort control resides at the top of the right-hand results column, aligned horizontally with the results-status-bar to create a clear control strip above the list. The right column begins roughly at the same vertical level as the midpoint of the left overlapping panel, so the sort control sits in the upper-right quadrant of the main content area. Within this strip, the sort control is right-aligned, occupying about the rightmost quarter of the results column width, while the status bar occupies the left side. There is a horizontal gap of 16–24px between the sort dropdown and the edge of the container, and a similar gap between it and the status text, keeping the area airy and scannable. The sort control has minimal height (32–40px), matching the compact, practical style of the site. A small vertical margin (16–24px) separates this control strip from the first result card below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the results column; it scrolls away with the results list as the user scrolls down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The locations results list occupies the full width of the right-hand column beneath the status bar and sort control. On large screens, this column spans about 55–60% of the content width and is aligned flush to the right edge of the max-width container, leaving a central gutter between it and the left panel. The list is a vertical stack of location cards, each card stretching almost the full width of this column with consistent side padding (16–24px) and vertical spacing between cards (16–24px). The top of the list starts roughly 24px below the control strip to maintain separation. The list naturally extends downward, becoming the main scrollable content area of the page; as the user scrolls, cards continue off the bottom while the header and hero remain off-screen. On tablet and mobile, the list shifts to full width and moves below the entire left panel, with the same vertical spacing, so results clearly follow the controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and vertically scrollable as part of the main page content; no fixed or sticky behavior. It simply grows in height as more results are loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each location result card is a rectangular block within the results list, designed to be clear and functional. It spans nearly the full width of the results column, inset from the column’s edges by 16–24px padding on all sides. Cards are arranged one per row in a single vertical column. The interior layout of each card follows a simple left-to-right structure: key information (location name, rating if applicable, address, and distance) is stacked in the left two-thirds, while primary actions (e.g., ‘View Details’, ‘Book Now’) align to the right, either as buttons or links, with consistent 8–16px spacing between them. Vertical spacing between textual elements inside the card uses the 8–16px scale, keeping content legible but compact. Cards are separated from each other by 16–24px of vertical whitespace, ensuring they feel like distinct units without overwhelming the page. A subtle border or shadow differentiates each card from the white background, consistent with the modest corporate style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the results list; cards are stacked and scroll vertically. No sticky or floating behavior at the card level.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results status bar sits at the very top of the right results column, forming the left side of the horizontal control strip just below the hero overlap. It is a slim horizontal area, about 32–40px tall, spanning roughly the left two-thirds of the results column’s width. Text such as “12 locations near 90210” or “No results for your filters” is left-aligned within this bar, with 16px padding from the left edge of the column. The background is either transparent or a very light tint that matches the overall palette, keeping it understated but readable. The sort control occupies the remaining right side of this same row, maintaining a gentle F-pattern where the eye reads status on the left then moves right to the sort options. A 16–24px gap below the bar visually separates it from the first result card, reinforcing its role as a summary indicator rather than part of the list itself.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and inline with the top of the results column; it scrolls out of view as the user scrolls down the list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state message appears in the same spatial slot as the locations-results-list when there are no matching locations. It is centered within the right column both horizontally and, where possible, vertically within the viewport area below the control strip. The message block occupies about 50–60% of the results column width, centered, with 24–32px padding and ample whitespace above and below (at least 48px from the control strip). If an illustration or icon is used, it sits above the main message text, centered, with 16px spacing between icon and text. Supplementary guidance or a ‘Clear filters’ button is placed beneath the primary message, aligned center, with 16px gaps. The left panel (search and filters) remains visible and unchanged, reinforcing that the user can adjust filters to restore results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the results column; it replaces the results list when active. It scrolls like normal content if its height exceeds the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading and error handler controls the feedback area within the right results column, over the same region where the list or empty state would appear. During loading, a spinner or progress indicator is centered horizontally in the results column, typically appearing 80–120px below the control strip to avoid feeling cramped, with optional dimming of existing content beneath if appropriate. For errors, an alert-style message block appears in place of the list, aligned similarly to the empty state: roughly center-aligned in the column, spanning about 50–60% of the column width, with clear message text and any retry actions stacked below. The vertical spacing around these states follows the same 24–32px margins used for other panels, maintaining consistency. The left panel remains intact, allowing users to adjust criteria even when errors occur.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlays or replaces the list area within the results column as needed, but still scrolls as part of the page content. No fixed or sticky behavior; the indicator’s position is relative to the top of the results column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state manager is non-visual and does not occupy any explicit on-screen space. Its effects are perceived indirectly: as users adjust the search form, toggles, filters, or sort options, the URL query parameters update to reflect the current state. This ensures that the layout and positions of visible components remain stable while allowing the page to be reloaded or shared with the same search context. It sits conceptually behind the scenes of the entire content area, coordinating state across the left panel and right results column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: No visual or spatial presence; it operates in the background without affecting physical layout or scroll behavior.

name: Location Detail
title: Location Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a specific automotive service location and routes users into the scheduling and coupons flows based on the selected location.

core_features: Load detailed information for a specific location using the locationId URL parameter and the getLocationDetail interface.
Display key location information including name, address, phone, activity status, average star rating, rating count, shuttle availability, and shuttle radius.
Show weekly business hours for the location, including closed days, based on LocationHour records returned by getLocationDetail.
List a summary of services available at the location, grouped or filterable by category, with starting price ranges for common services using services_summary.
Present location-specific featured coupons returned by getLocationDetail and allow navigation to the coupons page scoped to the same location.
Provide a primary call-to-action button that routes the user to the schedule_service.html page with the current locationId.
Handle loading and error states when the locationId is missing, invalid, or when getLocationDetail fails, and adjust available interactions accordingly.
user_workflows: View location details and schedule service: 1) User lands on location_detail.html with a locationId URL parameter. 2) The page calls getLocationDetail(locationId). 3) After successful load, the user reviews location info (address, phone, rating, hours, shuttle info). 4) User clicks the primary schedule button. 5) The browser navigates to schedule_service.html?locationId=<currentLocationId>.

Review services and starting prices: 1) User lands on location_detail.html with locationId. 2) getLocationDetail returns services_summary. 3) The services listing component displays each service with its category and starting price. 4) User optionally filters services by category using client-side controls. 5) User decides which type of service to book and clicks the main schedule button, continuing to schedule_service.html with the same locationId.

Check if shuttle is available: 1) User opens location_detail.html with locationId. 2) getLocationDetail returns shuttle_info and the Location entity fields offers_free_shuttle and shuttle_radius_miles. 3) The shuttle info component indicates whether a shuttle is offered and the applicable radius, if provided. 4) User interprets this when deciding their visit type before proceeding to scheduling.

Review location-specific coupons: 1) User opens location_detail.html with locationId. 2) getLocationDetail returns featured_coupons. 3) The coupons component lists these featured offers. 4) The user clicks an action such as a link to see all location-specific offers. 5) The browser navigates to coupons.html?locationId=<currentLocationId>.

Handle missing or invalid locationId: 1) User accesses location_detail.html without a locationId, or with a locationId for which getLocationDetail returns an error. 2) The loader component detects the missing/invalid parameter or failed response. 3) The page displays an error state explaining that the location cannot be loaded. 4) Schedule and coupons navigation controls are disabled or hidden because there is no valid location context.
interactions: On initial page load, the data loader component reads the locationId query parameter from the URL and triggers getLocationDetail(locationId).

If the locationId query parameter changes (for example via internal navigation that updates the URL), the data loader re-invokes getLocationDetail with the new locationId and refreshes all bound components.
Users can click the primary schedule button; this triggers construction of a URL schedule_service.html?locationId=<currentLocationId> and navigates the browser to that URL.
Users can click a link or button to view all location-specific offers; this constructs coupons.html?locationId=<currentLocationId> and navigates the browser there.

Users can interact with service category filter controls (such as a dropdown or tabbed selector) to filter the services_summary list; the filtering is done entirely client-side on the data returned by getLocationDetail without additional API calls.

Users can expand or collapse sections (such as hours, shuttle info, or coupons) to reveal or hide details; these interactions modify local UI state only and do not change the URL or make additional API calls.

If the location is marked inactive (is_active is false), the page may display a non-interactive state for the schedule button (e.g., disabled state) and an informational message, without attempting to navigate to scheduling.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state for this page is the locationId URL query parameter (e.g., location_detail.html?locationId=<id>). On page load or whenever the URL changes, the page reads locationId from the query string. If locationId is present, getLocationDetail(locationId) is called; if it is missing, the page enters an error state and does not attempt to load data. The response from getLocationDetail populates the in-memory page state: location (Location), hours (LocationHour[]), services_summary, shuttle_info, and featured_coupons (Coupon[]). All components subscribe to this shared state. If getLocationDetail fails or returns an empty/invalid location, the error state is set and components display appropriate messaging and disable navigation that depends on a valid locationId. The same locationId from the URL is reused when constructing outgoing navigation URLs for schedule_service.html and coupons.html so that the user remains scoped to the same location across flows. No state is persisted outside the URL and in-memory component state.

components.functionality: Reads the locationId query parameter from the URL, invokes getLocationDetail(locationId), manages loading and error states, and exposes the loaded data (location, hours, services_summary, shuttle_info, featured_coupons) to child components.

Displays core location information at the top of the page, including the location name, full address, phone number, average_rating, rating_count, and an activity status indicator derived from is_active. Provides quick access to tap-to-call (if supported by the platform) using the phone field.

Shows the weekly business hours for the location using the hours array. For each day_of_week, it displays open_time and close_time or indicates that the location is closed when is_closed is true. Optionally derives and shows whether the location is currently open based on the current date/time compared to today’s hours.

Displays whether the location offers a free shuttle service and, if available, the shuttle radius in miles. Uses shuttle_info from getLocationDetail along with the Location entity fields to provide a concise shuttle availability summary.

Provides client-side filtering controls for the services list based on service category (using category_slug and category_name). It generates the set of available categories from services_summary and allows the user to filter the services-summary-list without additional API calls.

Displays a list of services available at the location based on services_summary. For each service, shows service_name, category_name, and starting_price_from (if provided). Honors the active filters selected in services-summary-filters and displays only the matching services.

Lists the featured coupons for this location returned by getLocationDetail. For each Coupon, the component displays the name, category, discount_type, discount_value, and any supplementary fields such as code, description, and date-based validity if available. This list is informational on this page and does not apply coupons directly.

Provides a dedicated link or button that navigates the user to the coupons page, scoped to the current location. It constructs the URL coupons.html?locationId=<currentLocationId> using the locationId from the URL state.

Renders the primary call-to-action button for scheduling service at this location. When clicked and when the location is active, it navigates the user to schedule_service.html?locationId=<currentLocationId>. If the location is inactive or if there was an error loading the location, the button is disabled and an appropriate informational state is shown.

Displays status messages based on loader and location state, such as loading, error when getLocationDetail fails, missing locationId, or inactive location. It ensures users understand why the schedule or offers actions might be disabled.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-down, hero-plus-overlap pattern as the mockup. Below the global utility bar and main site header, a full-width hero image of the location signage spans the content width. Overlapping the lower-left area of this hero is a composite information block that stacks the location header summary, hours, and shuttle info in a single left-side column, leaving the right side of the hero mostly image. Beneath this overlapping block, the main content transitions into a two-column layout: the left column is dedicated to services filters and the services list, while the right column presents featured coupons and a link to more offers. A persistent schedule-service CTA bar anchors the bottom of the viewport, and status messages sit as a band between the location info and the services section.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-down, hero-plus-overlap pattern as the mockup. Below the global utility bar and main site header, a full-width hero image of the location signage spans the content width. Overlapping the lower-left area of this hero is a composite information block that stacks the location header summary, hours, and shuttle info in a single left-side column, leaving the right side of the hero mostly image. Beneath this overlapping block, the main content transitions into a two-column layout: the left column is dedicated to services filters and the services list, while the right column presents featured coupons and a link to more offers. A persistent schedule-service CTA bar anchors the bottom of the viewport, and status messages sit as a band between the location info and the services section.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport is consumed by the existing global utility/header (outside this layout) plus the very top of the hero image. The hero image itself occupies about 25–35% of the initial viewport height. The overlapping location info block (header summary + hours + shuttle) consumes the lower half of the hero’s height and sits over the left ~55–60% of the content width, leaving the right ~40–45% as exposed photo. The main services/coupons section beneath uses a 12-column grid: the services column spans about 7/12 of the width on the left, and the coupons/offers column spans about 5/12 on the right. Vertical spacing between sections is in the 32–48px range, using an 8px-based scale, with comfortable padding (16–24px) inside panels and cards. The schedule-service CTA bar spans full width (inside the max-width container) along the bottom edge of the viewport.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate and brochure-like. The overlapping location info block groups related information (summary, hours, shuttle) into separate subpanels with clear internal padding and line heights that favor readability over compactness. The services list and coupons present multiple items but with sufficient vertical spacing between cards and clear headings, avoiding any table-like or cramped appearance. Filters are kept in a single, clear row or multi-line stack with breathing room around controls. Status messages are concise banners, and the CTA bar has minimal, focused copy with a strong button, avoiding overload.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow preserves the F-pattern. The eye starts at the top-left (global logo, then location name within the overlapping card), moves across the top hero region, then down the left side through the stacked location info (header summary, hours, shuttle). From there, attention drops into the left services column: filters first, then the services list. After scanning the main text and service items, the eye naturally shifts right to the coupons column and the offers navigation link. The bright schedule-service CTA at the bottom edge of the viewport provides a strong, persistent call to action that the eye encounters frequently during scroll. Status banners, when present, create a brief horizontal interruption just before the services content, but remain secondary to the main flow.

layout.overall_description: Within the familiar corporate-style shell of the site, the location detail page opens with a wide hero image of the specific workshop or building signage, centered within a max-width container and framed by generous left and right margins. At the lower-left of this hero, a clean white overlapping card anchors the location content: the top portion highlights the location name and key contact details, while the area directly beneath is divided into clearly labeled hours and shuttle information panels. The card covers roughly the left three-fifths of the hero, leaving the right side open to the workshop photography for context. Below this overlapping block, the page transitions into a structured two-column section: on the left, a prominent “Services at this location” area combines filters and a vertically scrolling list of services; on the right, a narrower column stacks featured coupons and a link to view all offers. Throughout, spacing is measured and consistent, using the 8px-based scale so that panels feel substantial but not bulky. At the bottom of the viewport, a bold, yellow schedule-service CTA bar spans the width and remains visible as the user scrolls, acting as the primary action anchor. Occasional status messages appear as full-width banners between the overlapping location info and the services section, gently attracting attention without overwhelming the main layout. The overall composition mirrors the mockup’s hero-plus-overlap pattern and two-column balance, prioritizing clarity and trust while guiding the user steadily toward scheduling or using an offer.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When active, the location-detail-loader appears as a semi-transparent white overlay that covers the entire location detail content area, starting just below the main site header and extending down to just above the fixed schedule-service-cta bar. It spans the full width of the content container, aligned to the same left/right margins as the hero and main content, maintaining the centered 12-column grid. In the center of this overlay, a modest spinner or skeleton placeholder appears, approximately 64–96px in size, visually aligned with the center of the overlapping location info block on initial load. The underlying hero, cards, and lists are dimmed but remain faintly visible, communicating that content is loading without shifting the page layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned as an absolute or fixed overlay within the main content region; it sits above all other components in the stacking order while active, then disappears entirely once data has loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-header-summary forms the core of the hero treatment. It appears as a white or very light card that overlaps the lower-left quadrant of a full-width hero image of the location. The hero image fills the width of the max-width container and occupies roughly 30% of the initial viewport height. The summary card is aligned to the left margin of the content grid, spanning about 6–7 of the 12 columns (roughly 55–60% of the content width) and reaching from slightly above the vertical midpoint of the hero down past the hero’s bottom edge by about 24–32px, echoing the mockup’s overlapping content behavior. Inside the card, there is 24px padding on all sides.
