The location name appears as the primary heading at the top-left of the card, with address, phone, and key tags (e.g., ‘Services Provided at this Location’) arranged beneath in a left-to-right, top-to-bottom pattern. The right side of the hero, not covered by the card, continues to show the workshop/building photo, balancing the large text block on the left with imagery on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the page flow, but visually overlapping the hero image by using a negative top margin relative to the content section below; not sticky when scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-hours-panel sits immediately below the main text content of the location-header-summary, visually nested within the same overlapping card and sharing its background color. It occupies the full width of the card’s inner content area (the same 6–7 columns as the header summary) but is separated from the header summary by about 16–24px of vertical space and a subtle dividing line or heading. Internally, the hours panel uses 16–24px padding and presents its text in a left-aligned, stacked format with comfortable line height. On large screens, it occupies roughly the middle third of the card’s height, with shuttle-info-panel following beneath it. The left edge of the hours panel aligns exactly with the left grid margin of the header summary, reinforcing a single, strong left column that the user scans down from location name to hours. On smaller screens, it simply stacks underneath the header summary as one continuous block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the overlapping card; it inherits the card’s overlapping behavior over the hero but does not itself move or stick independently.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The shuttle-info-panel is placed directly beneath the location-hours-panel, still within the same overlapping card that extends over the bottom of the hero image. It also spans the full inner width of that card, maintaining the same left and right alignment as the hours panel. A vertical gap of roughly 16–24px separates it from the hours panel, giving each panel its own breathing room while visually grouping them as related location utilities. The shuttle section is formatted as a concise info block: a bold subheading such as ‘Shuttle Service’ at the top-left, with details (availability, radius, times) in shorter lines below. Together, the header summary, hours panel, and shuttle panel create a stacked column that occupies the left ~60% of the hero region, with the lower edge of the shuttle-info-panel slightly protruding into the main content area, reinforcing the layered overlap seen in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the overlapping card; it scrolls with the page and does not have its own fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The services-summary-filters component begins the main content section directly beneath the overlapping location info card. It is aligned with the same left and right margins as the card and sits about 32–40px below the bottom edge of the shuttle-info-panel, leaving a clear visual gap that marks the transition from hero/info to services. On large screens, the filters occupy the top portion of the left column in a two-column layout: they span approximately 7 of the 12 grid columns (around 60% of the width), starting at the left margin. Within this width, the filter controls (dropdowns, pills, or buttons) sit in a horizontal row that can wrap to a second line if needed, with 8–16px horizontal spacing between controls and 16–24px padding around the group. The height of this filters strip is relatively compact so it reads as a control bar rather than a large panel, but it’s given enough vertical padding to feel comfortable and clickable. The right column (for coupons) starts at the same vertical height, to the right of the filters, maintaining a clean horizontal alignment across the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content; it anchors the top of the left services column and scrolls off screen normally as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The services-summary-list is positioned directly below the services-summary-filters in the left column, forming the main vertical reading area of the page. It shares the same 7/12 column width as the filters and is left-aligned with them, creating a continuous column of content that runs from just below the hero down to the fold and beyond. A vertical gap of about 16–24px separates the list from the filters. Each service within the list is presented as a card or row with 16–24px internal padding and 16px vertical spacing between items, maintaining the moderate content density from the mockup. The list’s total height typically exceeds that of the coupons column on the right, so as the user scrolls, this left column becomes the primary content stream, with the right column visible alongside it near the top of the viewport. On narrower screens, the services list collapses to full width and stacks above the coupons section, but still preserves the generous vertical spacing between service items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and vertically scrollable within the page; it extends as tall as needed based on the number of services, forming the dominant scrolling column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-coupons-list occupies the right-hand column of the main content area, aligned horizontally with the top of the services-summary-filters. It spans approximately 5 of the 12 grid columns (about 40% of the content width) and is separated from the left services column by a gutter of around 24–32px, preserving clear two-column structure. Within this right column, coupon cards are stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing between them. Each coupon card has 16–24px internal padding and slightly bolder color accents (e.g., brand yellow highlights) to distinguish savings without overwhelming the overall corporate feel. The top edge of the first coupon card aligns with, or sits just below, the top edge of the filters strip, so that filters and coupons feel parallel at a glance. The list typically occupies less vertical space than the services list, so the column may end mid-way down the viewport while the services column continues. Beneath the final coupon card, room is left for the location-offers-navigation link, separated by about 16px of vertical space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within its column; it scrolls with the page and remains visually paired with the services column at higher scroll positions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-coupons-list occupies the right-hand column of the main content area, aligned horizontally with the top of the services-summary-filters. It spans approximately 5 of the 12 grid columns (about 40% of the content width) and is separated from the left services column by a gutter of around 24–32px, preserving clear two-column structure. Within this right column, coupon cards are stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing between them. Each coupon card has 16–24px internal padding and slightly bolder color accents (e.g., brand yellow highlights) to distinguish savings without overwhelming the overall corporate feel. The top edge of the first coupon card aligns with, or sits just below, the top edge of the filters strip, so that filters and coupons feel parallel at a glance. The list typically occupies less vertical space than the services list, so the column may end mid-way down the viewport while the services column continues. Beneath the final coupon card, room is left for the location-offers-navigation link, separated by about 16px of vertical space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within its column; it scrolls with the page and remains visually paired with the services column at higher scroll positions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-offers-navigation component sits directly beneath the featured-coupons-list in the same right-hand column. It is positioned with about 16–24px of vertical space below the last coupon card, and it is right-aligned within the 5-column width, reading as a concise link or small button such as ‘View all offers for this location.’ This placement allows it to act as a natural continuation of the coupons list: once users scan the featured coupons, their eye moves downward and slightly right to encounter this link. The component itself is low-profile—perhaps simple text with an arrow icon or a minimal ghost button—so it doesn’t visually compete with the main schedule-service-cta bar or the coupon cards, but it remains clearly clickable and noticeable as a next step.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column; it scrolls normally with the coupons list and does not stick to any viewport edge.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The schedule-service-cta is presented as a bold, full-width bar anchored along the bottom edge of the viewport, echoing the strong banded elements from the mockup’s top utility bar but with higher emphasis. It spans the full width of the content area (matching the max-width container) and is centered horizontally, with 16–24px of internal vertical padding and 24–32px horizontal padding on each side. The bar typically uses the brand’s strong yellow or a high-contrast color, with left-aligned text such as ‘Schedule service at [Location Name]’ and a prominent primary button aligned to the right side of the bar. On large screens, text and button are arranged in a single row; on smaller screens, they stack with the button centered. The bar sits above any browser UI and slightly above the absolute bottom of the page content, so as the user scrolls, it remains constant, subtly overlapping some of the lower page content but never obscuring critical text due to its modest height (roughly 56–72px).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed (sticky) to the bottom of the viewport once the page has loaded, remaining visible at all times as the user scrolls through the location details.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-status-messages component appears as a slim, full-width banner positioned between the overlapping location info card and the main services section. It spans the same max-width container as the other content and is aligned to the same left and right margins. Vertically, it sits about 16–24px below the bottom of the overlapping card and 16–24px above the services-summary-filters, creating a clearly separated band. The banner has a soft background color (e.g., pale yellow or blue) with left-aligned text describing the status (such as ‘Closed today’ or ‘Holiday hours in effect’). Its height is modest—often only a single line of text plus 8–16px of padding top and bottom—so it reads as an informational stripe rather than a full panel. If multiple messages are present, they may stack with 8px spacing between them within this band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the page flow; it scrolls away like normal content and does not stick to the top or bottom of the viewport.

name: Services & Categories
title: Services
description: Browse available automotive services by category, view brief descriptions and starting prices, and navigate to detailed service information or related coupons.

core_features: Load all active service categories using the getServiceCategories interface and present them as selectable category filters.
Determine the initially selected category based on the optional "category" URL parameter or default to a valid category when the parameter is missing or invalid.
Load services for the currently selected category using the getServicesByCategory interface and display each service with its name, brief description, and starting price.
Allow users to switch categories, updating the URL "category" parameter and reloading the services list for the newly selected category.
Enable navigation to the Service Detail page when a specific service is clicked, passing the selected service identifier via URL parameter.
Provide a link to view coupons related to the currently selected category, navigating to the coupons page with the appropriate "category" URL parameter.
Handle loading, empty, and error states for both category and service data without using any hardcoded data values.
user_workflows: Browse services by category: 1) User opens services.html. 2) Page-state logic reads the "category" URL parameter (if present). 3) Page calls getServiceCategories to retrieve all active categories. 4) Page selects the current category based on the URL parameter if it matches a retrieved category; otherwise it selects a default category from the returned list. 5) Page calls getServicesByCategory with the selected category's slug. 6) Page displays the list of services for that category, including name, description, and starting price. 7) User can click another category tab to repeat steps 5–6 for the new category.

View service details from the list: 1) User locates a service in the list for the current category. 2) User clicks the service item. 3) Page navigates to service_detail.html with the clicked service's identifier set as the "serviceId" URL parameter. 4) The Service Detail page (out of scope here) uses that parameter to show full details and availability.

Access category-specific coupons: 1) User selects a category (either initially or by clicking a category tab). 2) The currently selected category slug is known to the page state. 3) User clicks the "View coupons for this category" link. 4) Page navigates to coupons.html with the selected category slug set as the "category" URL parameter, allowing the coupons page to present relevant offers.

Use browser navigation with category state: 1) User opens services.html and selects several different categories in sequence. 2) Each category change updates the URL "category" parameter. 3) User presses the browser Back or Forward buttons. 4) Page listens for URL changes, re-derives the selected category from the updated "category" parameter, and calls getServicesByCategory for that category so the visible category and service list always match the URL.
interactions: Click on a category filter/tab: Updates the selected category in page state, updates the "category" URL parameter via history API, and triggers a call to getServicesByCategory with the new category slug.
Click on a service item in the list: Reads that service's identifier from the bound data and navigates to service_detail.html?serviceId={serviceId}.
Click on the "View coupons for this category" link: Reads the current selected category slug from page state and navigates to coupons.html?category={selectedCategorySlug}.

Initial page load: Triggers parsing of the "category" URL parameter, fetching of categories via getServiceCategories, selection of the initial category, and a subsequent call to getServicesByCategory for that category.

Retry after error: If category or service loading fails, the user can click a retry action which re-invokes the appropriate interface (getServiceCategories or getServicesByCategory) using the current category state.

Browser Back/Forward navigation: When the URL changes and the "category" parameter differs from the current state, the page updates the selected category in state, visually updates the active category tab, and calls getServicesByCategory for the slug from the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page relies on the "category" URL query parameter to represent the currently selected service category. On initial load, the page reads this parameter. After calling getServiceCategories, the page checks whether the parameter value matches the slug of any returned active category. If it does, that category becomes the selected category; if it does not or if the parameter is absent, a default category is selected from the returned list (for example, the first active category). The selected category slug is then used as the parameter for getServicesByCategory to fetch the services list. Whenever the user clicks on a different category tab, the page updates the selected category in memory and updates the "category" parameter in the URL using the browser history API without reloading the page. This change triggers a new call to getServicesByCategory with the updated slug. The page also listens for browser history navigation events (such as Back and Forward); when detected, it re-parses the current URL, derives the "category" parameter, and if it differs from the current selected category, it updates the page state and re-fetches services via getServicesByCategory. No persistent client storage (such as localStorage) is used; all state beyond the URL parameters is maintained only in page memory while the page is open.

components.functionality: Coordinates data loading and high-level state for the Services page. On initialization, it reads the "category" URL parameter, calls getServiceCategories to retrieve all active categories, determines the selected category based on the URL parameter or a fallback rule, and then calls getServicesByCategory for the selected category. It stores in-memory page state including: list of categories, selected category slug, services list for the selected category, loading flags and error flags for both category and services data. It also exposes handlers for category selection changes, retry actions, and URL-change reactions.
data_binding: Service (via serialized data within getServicesByCategory response)

components.functionality: Displays the list of active service categories as selectable tabs or filters. Highlights the currently selected category, based on the selected category slug provided by the page-state-manager. When the user clicks a category tab, it invokes the onCategoryChange handler, passing the clicked category's slug so that the page-state-manager can update URL state and reload services.

Renders the list of services returned by getServicesByCategory for the currently selected category. For each service item, it displays the service name, a brief description, and the starting price value provided in the starting_price_from field. It also handles empty results by deferring to the empty-state component when no services are returned.
data_binding: Service (via each element of getServicesByCategory response)
Selected category slug (for contextual messaging if needed)

components.functionality: Represents a single service entry within the services list. It receives a service object from the getServicesByCategory response, including the service_id, name, description, and starting_price_from fields. When clicked, it uses the service_id to navigate programmatically to service_detail.html, passing the serviceId URL parameter.

Provides a context-aware navigation link from the current category view to the coupons page. It reads the selected category slug from page state and builds a URL to coupons.html with the "category" query parameter set accordingly. When clicked, it performs navigation to that URL so the coupons page can filter by the same category.

Displays a loading state while the list of categories is being fetched via getServiceCategories. It becomes visible when the page-state-manager sets a "categoriesLoading" flag and hides when the categories load successfully or fail.
data_binding: Categories loading state flag (primitive state value)

components.functionality: Displays a loading state while services for the selected category are being fetched via getServicesByCategory. It becomes visible when the page-state-manager sets a "servicesLoading" flag and hides when the services load successfully or fail.
data_binding: Services loading state flag (primitive state value)
Selected category slug (for contextual messaging if desired)

components.functionality: Shows an error message and a retry control when the call to getServiceCategories fails. It is displayed based on an error flag set by the page-state-manager. The retry control triggers another call to getServiceCategories via the onRetryCategoriesLoad handler.
data_binding: Categories error state flag (primitive state value)

components.functionality: Shows an error message and a retry control when the call to getServicesByCategory for the current category fails. It is displayed based on an error flag set by the page-state-manager. The retry control triggers another call to getServicesByCategory with the currently selected category slug via the onRetryServicesLoad handler.
data_binding: Services error state flag (primitive state value)

components.functionality: Appears when getServicesByCategory returns an empty list for the selected category and there is no error. It communicates that no services are currently available for the selected category and may suggest trying a different category. It is controlled by checking that services have finished loading, the error flag is clear, and the services list length is zero.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-to-bottom structure as the mockup: slim yellow utility bar at the very top, then the white header with logo and centered navigation, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/building photo and a bold left-aligned title like “Services & Categories”. Directly overlapping the lower third of the hero is the main content block: on the left, a light, semi-transparent panel that contains the category tabs, list of services, and related state messages; on the right, a photo of a mechanic working under the hood. All interactive components (tabs, list, link, states) live inside the left text panel, maintaining a clear two-column 6/6 or 7/5 split between content and imagery.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-to-bottom structure as the mockup: slim yellow utility bar at the very top, then the white header with logo and centered navigation, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/building photo and a bold left-aligned title like “Services & Categories”. Directly overlapping the lower third of the hero is the main content block: on the left, a light, semi-transparent panel that contains the category tabs, list of services, and related state messages; on the right, a photo of a mechanic working under the hood. All interactive components (tabs, list, link, states) live inside the left text panel, maintaining a clear two-column 6/6 or 7/5 split between content and imagery.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header and utility bar together occupy about the top 10–15% of the viewport. The hero occupies roughly 25–35% of the viewport height, with the main content panel overlapping its lower edge by about 64px (8×8). Below the overlap, the left content panel (housing tabs and services list) takes up about 55–60% of the content width, while the right mechanic-photo column takes 40–45%. The main content container is centered with generous horizontal margins (roughly 80–120px on large screens). Vertical spacing between the hero heading and the overlapping content panel is moderate, aligned to the 32–48px scale, and inner padding of all content blocks uses 16–24px to keep a solid but not cramped brochure-like feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate: category tabs sit in a single row with comfortable horizontal spacing; the services list is a single column of full-width cards stacked vertically, each with a clear title, short description, and starting price. Items are separated by either light dividers or 16–24px vertical gaps. Status indicators (loading, error, empty) occupy the same zones as their related content but with generous whitespace around the messages so the layout never feels crowded. Typography remains straightforward and readable, with room around each block following the 8px spacing scale.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow keeps the F-pattern from the mockup: the eye starts at the logo and navigation, then moves across the top bar and down to the hero title on the left. From there it drops into the overlapping left content panel, first encountering the category tabs near the top, then scanning downward through the services list items. The right mechanic photo acts as a large secondary visual anchor but does not compete with the text; it reinforces context. Within the left column, the flow is simple: category tabs row at the top, optional category coupons link at the far right of that row, then the body area where loading/error/empty states or the services list appear. Error messages and empty states are centered within their respective areas, momentarily capturing attention without breaking the overall left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading order.

layout.overall_description: The page opens with a slim yellow utility bar stretching full width across the very top, followed immediately by a clean white header containing the garage’s logo on the left, simple horizontal navigation centered, and a social or contact icon anchored on the right. Beneath this, a full-width hero/banner image of the workshop or building signage fills roughly the next third of the screen, with a bold white heading like “Services & Categories” positioned in the left-middle of the image. Overlapping the bottom edge of this hero by about 64px, a centered content container appears: on the left, a light, slightly translucent panel with soft corners or a subtle shadow houses all of the interactive service content; on the right, a large photo of a mechanic working under the hood aligns to the right edge of the container, completing a 6/6 or 7/5 two-column split. Inside the left panel, a horizontal row of category tabs sits at the top, spanning most of the panel width, with a small “View coupons for this category” link tucked into the upper right of the same row. Directly beneath the tabs, the main body area of the panel is reserved for either loading indicators, error or empty messages, or a vertically stacked single-column list of service cards, each describing an individual service with its starting price and a small navigational affordance. Generous vertical spacing separates each item, and the entire left panel uses consistent 16–24px padding on all sides. The right column’s large mechanic image stays mostly static and acts as a contextual backdrop, while the left panel scrolls normally with the page, preserving the clear, practical small-business aesthetic of the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-state-manager is a non-visual structural component that sits logically around the main content area underneath the hero and overlapping section. It wraps the category-tabs row, the services-list region, the category-coupons-link, and all loading/error/empty states, controlling which of these is visible at any given time without altering the page’s spatial structure. Visually, the layout remains the same: the left overlapping panel still occupies about 55–60% of the content width and aligns vertically with the right mechanic-image column; the state-manager simply determines whether the services-list region shows service-list-item cards or a status indicator message. It does not add any spacing or visual blocks of its own and follows the same content container width centered within the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual wrapper that does not occupy its own space; it conditionally shows/hides child components (tabs, list, status indicators) while preserving their positions within the left overlapping content panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The category-tabs component is placed at the very top of the left overlapping content panel, directly beneath the lower edge of the hero image where the panel begins. It stretches horizontally across roughly 80–90% of the panel’s width, starting from the left inner padding edge and stopping a bit before the right edge to leave room for the category-coupons-link. The tabs are arranged in a single row, left-aligned, with each tab sized to its label and separated by 16px horizontal gaps. The row sits on a subtle horizontal baseline about 16–24px below the panel’s top edge, leaving a consistent top padding aligned to the 8px spacing scale. Vertically, the tabs occupy a relatively shallow band—about 48–56px in height—so they feel functional and understated, not oversized. There is about 16–24px of vertical space below the tabs row before the services-list or any status indicator begins, maintaining a breathable separation between the filter controls and the content they affect.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the page scroll as a standard block at the top of the left content column; it is not sticky or fixed, but consistently remains the first interactive element encountered within the panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The services-list component occupies the main body area beneath the category-tabs within the left overlapping panel. It starts approximately 16–24px below the tabs row and spans nearly the full width of the panel between the left and right inner padding (about 88–92% of the panel’s width), leaving uniform margins on both sides. Vertically, it fills most of the remaining space of the first viewport below the hero and then continues downward as the user scrolls, creating a vertical stack of service-list-item cards. Each list item is full-width within this column, with 16–24px of vertical space between cards to maintain a moderate, brochure-like density. When populated, the list area pushes downward, stopping several hundred pixels above the bottom of the mechanic photo on typical laptop screens, so the right image and left list appear visually balanced. On narrower screens, the services-list remains a single column and naturally reflows beneath the category-tabs within the same container width.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level content region that expands vertically with its items; it shares its area with loading, error, and empty states (only one visible at a time), but always remains positioned directly under the tabs within the left panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each service-list-item is displayed as a full-width card within the services-list column. The card aligns flush with the left and right inner padding of the left panel, occupying roughly 100% of the list’s available width. It has internal padding of about 16–24px on all sides, following the base spacing system, and sits on a light background (either white or a slightly tinted surface) that contrasts subtly with the panel’s underlying tone. The top of each card begins with the service title left-aligned, followed by a short 2–4 line description below it, and a starting price aligned to the right of the card or placed in the lower right corner. A small navigational affordance (such as a right-pointing chevron) may appear inline with the title or at the far right, reinforcing that each card can be clicked for details. Vertically, each card is separated from the next by about 16–24px of whitespace or a subtle dividing line, ensuring the list feels structured but not cramped. The text layout echoes the mockup’s paragraph spacing with comfortable line height and no dense elements, keeping the reading experience similar to a printed service brochure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Repeating block element within the services-list; its width is constrained by the left panel, and it stacks vertically without overlapping other items. Cards adapt their height to content but maintain consistent padding and separation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The category-coupons-link is positioned on the same horizontal line as the category-tabs, aligned to the far right within the left overlapping panel. It sits within the top control row, sharing the vertical space but visually separated: while the tabs cluster starting at the left edge of the panel’s inner padding, the coupons link is anchored near the right inner padding edge, roughly 8–16px in from the panel’s right border. This creates a gentle, balanced stretch across the width of the panel’s header row, echoing the top header where navigation is centered and a social icon is pushed to the right. The link’s text is small to medium-sized, styled as an inline text link or subtle button, with a single-line height that keeps it visually lighter than the tabs themselves. There is about 8px of vertical breathing room above and below the text within the row, aligning with the 48–56px overall height of the tabs row. When the coupons link is not applicable, its space collapses, allowing the tabs row to continue using the full width without leaving awkward gaps.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline element anchored to the right within the tabs header row; it scrolls with the content and does not affect the vertical stacking order of other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading-indicator-categories appears in place of the category-tabs when category data is being fetched. It occupies the same horizontal band at the top of the left overlapping panel, starting about 16–24px below the panel’s top edge and spanning roughly the same width the tabs would occupy (around 80–90% of the panel). The indicator is centered vertically within this band and left-aligned with where the first tab would normally appear, often as a small spinner icon followed by concise loading text. Surrounding whitespace matches the 8px-based spacing, with about 16px of space above and below, preserving the calm, uncluttered look. The services-list region beneath remains in its usual position but may also be replaced by the services loading indicator if both are loading simultaneously.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces the category-tabs within their reserved row; once loading completes, this indicator is removed and the tabs appear in the same location without shifting other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading-indicator-services appears within the main body area of the left overlapping panel, exactly where the services-list would normally be. It is positioned about 16–24px beneath the tabs row (or beneath the category loading indicator if both are active), centered horizontally within the panel’s content width. The indicator might be a spinner or progress icon with a brief “Loading services…” label, occupying a compact vertical block of about 64–96px height, while leaving generous empty space above and below so it doesn’t feel congested. The right mechanic image remains visible, giving the page a stable visual anchor while the user waits. The placement keeps the user’s attention anchored in the main reading column, consistent with the F-pattern flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces the services-list content within its allocated region; it does not alter the size of the surrounding left panel, only what is displayed inside that vertical space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-state-categories component is shown in the same top control band where the category-tabs normally appear. It occupies this horizontal strip beneath the top edge of the left overlapping panel, spanning roughly 80–90% of the panel width, starting from the left inner padding. Within this band, the error message is left-aligned but may include a small warning icon and a short, clear error sentence, possibly with a retry button placed immediately to its right. Vertical padding of 8–12px above and below the text keeps the band similar in height to the original tabs row, so the layout doesn’t jump. The services-list area below remains in its standard position but may show its own service-related error or empty indicator, meaning the user sees a stacked set of messages: category error at the top, then whatever is relevant for services beneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Replaces the tabs row within its reserved strip at the top of the left panel. It scrolls normally with the page and collapses back to the standard tabs layout once the error is resolved or categories are successfully reloaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-state-services component appears in the main services content area directly under the tabs (or category error/loading state). It sits approximately 16–24px below the control row and is centered horizontally within the left panel, but its text is left-aligned to match the overall reading flow. The error block has comfortable padding (16–24px) around the message, and may show a small icon followed by a concise error description and a retry action. It occupies a moderate vertical space—roughly the height of one or two service cards—ensuring it is noticeable without feeling overly heavy. When visible, no service-list-items are shown; the right mechanic image remains untouched, continuing to provide visual context. The space below the error message is open, with at least 32px of margin before any subsequent sections or footer content, preventing the message from feeling cramped at the bottom of the panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces the entire services-list region. It shares the same boundaries and scroll behavior as the list; when the user retries and data loads successfully, the error block is removed and replaced by the stacked list of service-list-items in the same position.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty-state-services component also occupies the services-list area beneath the category-tabs. It is displayed when a category has no available services to show. Positioned about 16–24px under the tabs or category state, it is centered horizontally in the left panel and vertically biased toward the upper half of the panel area so the user sees it immediately without scrolling. The empty state message is short and clear, potentially accompanied by a subtle icon (such as an outline of a car with a dashed box) and a gentle suggestion, like changing categories or checking back later. The block uses generous padding (24–32px within) and at least 32–48px of free space beneath, aligning with the moderate density of the brochure-like layout. No service-list-item cards appear when this state is visible, so the area feels intentionally sparse rather than broken.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Occupies the same spatial region as the services-list, fully replacing its content while maintaining the same top offset and horizontal width within the left panel. It scrolls with the content and collapses back to the normal list layout when services become available.

name: Service Detail & Availability
title: Service Detail & Availability
description: Displays detailed information for a selected automotive service and lets users search nearby locations for availability on a chosen date.

core_features: Load and display detailed information for the service identified by the serviceId URL parameter using getServiceDetail.
Show typical duration, detailed description, service category, and starting price for the selected service.
Display available service packages (variants) for the selected service and allow the user to select a package.
Provide a ZIP code and date search form for checking which locations offer the selected service or service package on a specific date.
Use searchServiceAvailability to retrieve locations, prices, and available appointment time slots based on serviceId/servicePackageId, ZIP, date, and filters.
Allow users to filter location results by price, closing time, distance, and shuttle availability.
Let users click a location name to open location_detail.html with the appropriate locationId URL parameter.
Let users click "View Times" or "Book" for a selected location to navigate to schedule_service.html with locationId, serviceId, and date in the URL.
user_workflows:
1) View service details:
   - User lands on service_detail.html with a serviceId in the URL.
   - On page load, the page calls getServiceDetail(serviceId).
   - The page displays the service name, category, typical duration, detailed description, and starting price.
   - If the service has multiple packages, the default package is preselected in the package selector component.

2) Select a different service package (when available):
   - User opens the service package selector and chooses a different package from the list returned by getServiceDetail.popular_packages.
   - The page updates the selected servicePackageId in the internal state and in the URL parameters.
   - If ZIP and date are already present in the URL, the page automatically re-runs searchServiceAvailability with the updated servicePackageId and refreshes the results list.

3) Search for locations by ZIP and date:
   - User enters a ZIP code and selects a date in the availability search form.
   - User submits the form.
   - The page updates the URL parameters (zip, date, servicePackageId if selected, and resets filter-related parameters to defaults).
   - The page calls searchServiceAvailability with serviceId (always), servicePackageId (if selected), the entered zip, selected date, and the current filters.
   - The page displays a list of locations with their name, distance, closing time for that date, free shuttle indicator, lowest price, and available time slots (if returned).

4) Refine location results with filters:
   - After search results appear, the user adjusts filters such as price range, closing time, distance, or shuttle availability in the filters component.
   - On any filter change or filter form submit, the page updates the corresponding filter parameters in the URL and re-calls searchServiceAvailability with the updated filters while keeping serviceId, servicePackageId, zip, and date unchanged.
   - The locations list refreshes to reflect the filtered results.

5) View location details:
   - In the results list, the user clicks a location name.
   - The page navigates to location_detail.html, adding the locationId of the clicked result as a URL parameter.

6) Start booking at a specific location:
   - In the results list, the user clicks a "View Times" or "Book" action for a specific location.
   - The page reads the currently selected date and serviceId from the page state/URL.
   - The page navigates to schedule_service.html with URL parameters: locationId (from the selected result), serviceId (from the current page), and date (from the current search).

7) Handling quote-only services:
   - When getServiceDetail indicates is_quote_only is true, the page still displays service details but does not display bookable availability if the business rules require a separate quote flow.
   - The search form may be disabled or replaced with instructional text indicating that booking must be initiated via the appropriate flow on another page (navigation behavior for that flow is defined outside this page).
interactions: On initial page load with serviceId URL parameter: trigger getServiceDetail to fetch service details.
On missing or invalid serviceId: display an error state or redirect logic defined outside this document (page should not attempt to call getServiceDetail without a valid serviceId).
On package selection change: update selected servicePackageId in state and URL; if zip and date are present, re-run searchServiceAvailability.
On ZIP input change: update local form state only (do not call search until form submit).
On date selection change: update local form state only (do not call search until form submit).

On availability search form submit: validate that ZIP and date are present; update URL parameters; call searchServiceAvailability with serviceId, optional servicePackageId, zip, date, and current filter values.
On filter value change (price, closing time, distance, shuttle availability, sort order): update filter parameters in URL; call searchServiceAvailability with the updated filters.
On click of a location name in the results list: navigate to location_detail.html with locationId parameter set to the clicked location's id.
On click of "View Times" for a location: navigate to schedule_service.html with URL parameters locationId, serviceId, and date using the current selections.

On click of "Book" for a location (if both buttons exist or if a single primary action is used): perform the same navigation as "View Times" to schedule_service.html with locationId, serviceId, and date.

On URL change (e.g., browser back/forward): re-read URL parameters (serviceId, zip, date, servicePackageId, filter params) and update page state; if zip and date are present, re-run searchServiceAvailability to restore the previous search state.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page relies on URL parameters as the single source of truth for state that must persist across navigation or browser history changes.

- Required parameter:
  - serviceId: Identifies which service's detail to show. On page load, if serviceId is present, the page calls getServiceDetail(serviceId). The page does not attempt to function without a valid serviceId.

- Optional search parameters:
  - zip: If present, represents the user's selected ZIP code for availability search. When both zip and date are present, the page automatically calls searchServiceAvailability using the current filters.
  - date: Represents the selected service date. Stored and passed as a URL-safe date format. When both zip and date are present, the page calls searchServiceAvailability.
  - servicePackageId: If present, indicates which package of the selected service is currently active. When this parameter changes (e.g., via package selector), the page updates the package selection display and, if zip and date exist, re-runs searchServiceAvailability.

- Filter-related URL parameters (names are implementation-defined but must be consistently used):
  - filterMaxPrice: Maximum price filter applied to the search. When changed, triggers a new searchServiceAvailability call with updated filters.
  - filterMinClosingTime or filterClosingTime: Represents a constraint for locations closing after a certain time. A change triggers a new searchServiceAvailability call.
  - filterMaxDistance: Limits results to locations within a certain distance in miles. A change triggers a new searchServiceAvailability call.
  - filterFreeShuttle: Boolean flag indicating whether to show only locations that offer_free_shuttle. A change triggers a new searchServiceAvailability call.
  - sortOrder: Optional parameter to indicate sort preference (e.g., by price, distance, rating). Used when constructing the filters object for searchServiceAvailability.

- Derived state from URL:
  - On every page load and on browser navigation events, the page parses all URL parameters and reconstructs the current state (selected package, search criteria, filters).
  - If zip and date are missing, no availability search is executed automatically; the page shows the service details and prompts the user to run a search.
  - If zip and date are present but the user changes the package selection, the page updates servicePackageId in the URL and re-runs searchServiceAvailability to keep results consistent with the chosen package.

- Interface usage in state transitions:
  - getServiceDetail is called only with the serviceId URL parameter and its response populates service details and available packages.
  - searchServiceAvailability is called whenever a complete search state exists (serviceId, zip, date, and the current filters and optional servicePackageId). The filters URL parameters are mapped into the 'filters' argument for the interface call.

All persistent user choices that affect data retrieval (service package, ZIP, date, filters, sort) are mirrored in URL parameters to allow sharable URLs and correct behavior with browser navigation.

components.functionality: On page initialization or when the serviceId URL parameter changes, this component calls getServiceDetail and exposes the loaded data to other components.
event_handlers: onInit: Read serviceId from URL; if present, call getServiceDetail({ serviceId }).
onUrlChange: If serviceId has changed, re-call getServiceDetail({ serviceId }).

onServiceDetailLoaded: Store the returned service object, typical_duration_minutes, detailed_description, starting_price_from, is_quote_only, category_name, category_slug, and popular_packages in page-level state accessible to child components.

components.functionality: Displays the main information about the selected service, including service name, category, typical duration, and starting price, based on the data returned from getServiceDetail.
event_handlers: onServiceDetailUpdated: Update displayed title, category name, typical duration, detailed description, and starting price whenever the service-detail-loader provides new data.

components.functionality: Shows the detailed_description of the service and any additional text-based information returned by getServiceDetail.
event_handlers: onServiceDetailUpdated: Render or update the detailed_description content.

components.functionality: Displays and manages the selection of service packages for the current service, using the popular_packages array from getServiceDetail and reflecting the selected package in URL state.
event_handlers: onServiceDetailUpdated: Populate the list of selectable service packages using popular_packages and preselect the package that matches the servicePackageId URL parameter or the package marked as is_default if no URL parameter is set.
onPackageChange: When the user selects a package, update the servicePackageId URL parameter and internal state.
onPackageChange: If zip and date URL parameters are already present, trigger the availability-search-manager to re-run searchServiceAvailability with the new servicePackageId.

components.functionality: Indicates when the service is quote-only by inspecting the is_quote_only flag from getServiceDetail, and controls whether booking-related components are enabled or disabled based on business rules.
event_handlers: onServiceDetailUpdated: If is_quote_only is true, display a quote-only message and inform the availability-search-form and availability-results-list components to disable or hide booking actions accordingly; if false, ensure booking components are enabled.

components.functionality: Allows the user to enter a ZIP code and select a date to search for locations offering the selected service or service package.
event_handlers: onInit: Read existing zip and date URL parameters and prefill the form fields if they are present.
onZipChange: Update local form state with the new ZIP value (no API call yet).
onDateChange: Update local form state with the new date value (no API call yet).

onSubmit: Validate that both ZIP and date are present; update URL parameters zip and date (and servicePackageId if a package is selected) and reset filter-related URL parameters to their default state; then trigger availability-search-manager to call searchServiceAvailability.

components.functionality: Provides controls for refining search results by price, closing time, distance, shuttle availability, and sort order. Translates user-selected filter values into URL parameters and searchServiceAvailability filter arguments.
event_handlers: onInit: Read filter-related URL parameters (filterMaxPrice, filterClosingTime, filterMaxDistance, filterFreeShuttle, sortOrder) and initialize filter controls to match.

onFilterChange: Whenever any filter value changes, update the corresponding URL parameters and trigger availability-search-manager to re-run searchServiceAvailability using the current serviceId, optional servicePackageId, zip, date, and updated filters.
onResetFilters: Clear filter-related URL parameters and trigger a new searchServiceAvailability call with filters cleared.

components.functionality: Central controller for calling searchServiceAvailability whenever search criteria or filters change, and distributing the results to the results list and related components.
event_handlers: onSearchCriteriaChange: When serviceId, zip, date, servicePackageId, or filters in URL parameters change, determine if both zip and date are present; if so, construct the filters object from URL parameters and call searchServiceAvailability({ serviceId, servicePackageId (optional), zip, date, filters }).

onSearchResultsLoaded: Store the returned array of locations (each with location, location_id, name, distance_miles, today_close_time, offers_free_shuttle, shuttle_radius_miles, lowest_price, currency, available_time_slots) in shared state for the availability-results-list and availability-summary components.
onSearchError: Expose an error state to be displayed by the availability-results-list or a dedicated error component.
onNoResults: Expose a no-results state if the searchServiceAvailability response contains an empty array.

components.functionality: Displays high-level information about the current search, such as the number of locations found and the active search criteria (zip, date, and selected package).
event_handlers: onSearchResultsLoaded: Count the number of locations in the response and display this along with the current zip, date, and selected package name.
onNoResults: Display a message indicating no locations matched the current criteria.

components.functionality: Displays a list of locations returned by searchServiceAvailability, including pricing, hours, shuttle availability, and available time slots, and provides actions to view location details or start booking.
event_handlers: onSearchResultsLoaded: Render a list where each item uses properties from the searchServiceAvailability response: name, distance_miles, today_close_time, offers_free_shuttle, shuttle_radius_miles, lowest_price, currency, and available_time_slots.
onLocationNameClick(locationId): Navigate to location_detail.html with URL parameter locationId set to the clicked location's id.
onViewTimesClick(locationId): Read serviceId and date from the current URL parameters; navigate to schedule_service.html with locationId, serviceId, and date parameters.
onBookClick(locationId): Perform the same navigation as onViewTimesClick, ensuring the selected date is passed to schedule_service.html.

onQuoteOnlyModeUpdated: If the quote-only-indicator indicates that booking should not be available, hide or disable the View Times and Book actions while still displaying location information if allowed.

components.functionality: Within each location result, optionally displays a summarized view of available time slots returned by searchServiceAvailability to inform users before they navigate to the full scheduling page.
event_handlers: onResultItemRender: If available_time_slots are present for a location, display a subset or summary of these items (e.g., earliest and latest available times) according to page rules; if not present, show a textual indicator that times can be viewed on the next page.

onQuoteOnlyModeUpdated: If booking is disabled due to quote-only mode, adjust display of time slots accordingly (e.g., showing that times represent potential quote appointment windows rather than direct bookings, according to business rules defined elsewhere).

components.functionality: Handles loading, error, and empty states for both service detail and availability search operations.
event_handlers: onServiceDetailLoading: Display a loading state for the service detail section while getServiceDetail is in progress.
onServiceDetailError: Display an error message if getServiceDetail fails.
onSearchLoading: Display a loading state for the availability results while searchServiceAvailability is in progress.
onSearchError: Display an error message if searchServiceAvailability fails.
onNoResults: Show a specific empty state message for no availability results.

components.functionality: Keeps the page's internal state synchronized with URL parameters for serviceId, zip, date, servicePackageId, and all filters, and propagates changes to data-loader components.
event_handlers: onInit: Parse all relevant URL parameters (serviceId, zip, date, servicePackageId, filter parameters, sortOrder) and initialize shared state.

onUserStateChange: When the user changes package, search criteria, or filters, update the URL parameters accordingly without reloading the page, then notify dependent components (service-detail-loader, availability-search-manager, availability-filter-bar).
onBrowserNavigation: Detect back/forward navigation and re-parse the URL to restore the previous state and re-trigger any necessary API calls (getServiceDetail, searchServiceAvailability).

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom structure: a global utility bar and header/nav sit above this page’s content. The service detail area mimics the hero + overlapping two‑column layout: a full‑width workshop/building hero image with the service heading on the left, then an overlapping left text panel for the detailed service description and package selector, with a mechanic/workshop image visually balancing on the right. Beneath this, a clearly separated availability section spans the central content column: search form first, then a slim filter bar, followed by availability summary and a vertical list of result cards. Data loaders and URL state sync are non‑visual and simply orchestrate these visible zones.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom structure: a global utility bar and header/nav sit above this page’s content. The service detail area mimics the hero + overlapping two‑column layout: a full‑width workshop/building hero image with the service heading on the left, then an overlapping left text panel for the detailed service description and package selector, with a mechanic/workshop image visually balancing on the right. Beneath this, a clearly separated availability section spans the central content column: search form first, then a slim filter bar, followed by availability summary and a vertical list of result cards. Data loaders and URL state sync are non‑visual and simply orchestrate these visible zones.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport is occupied by the utility bar and global header (outside this spec). The hero + service header area takes about 25–30% of the initial viewport height with a full‑width background image. The overlapping service description panel and right‑side mechanic image together fill the remaining visible height of the first screen: the left 55–60% of the content width is the text panel, the right 40–45% is the photo. Below this, the availability section uses the full central content width (a centered container at roughly 1,120–1,200px with 80–120px side margins). Vertical spacing between major sections (hero → overlapping description → availability search) follows 32–48px increments, while internal paddings in panels and cards are around 16–24px.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate and brochure‑like. The service description offers multiple paragraphs with generous line height and 16–24px spacing between blocks. The package selector uses clear, separated options rather than dense tables. The availability search and filters are arranged in a single, easy‑to‑scan row (wrapping on small screens) with comfortable gaps between fields. Each availability result card has enough padding and vertical spacing (24px padding, 16–24px gaps) so information is legible without feeling sparse, matching the practical, small‑business corporate feel of the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows the mockup’s F‑pattern: the eye starts at the top‑left logo and nav, then drops to the large service heading in the hero on the left. From there it flows down into the overlapping service description panel, reading the heading and paragraphs first. The right‑side mechanic image acts as a secondary pull, reinforcing context without competing with the text. As the user scrolls, the flow continues straight down to the availability search form, then the filter bar, the compact availability summary, and finally the vertical stack of results, each read left‑to‑right (workshop info) and then across to the right‑aligned times. Color and hierarchy (strong blue/yellow accents on headings, CTAs, and status indicators) support this left‑anchored, top‑down scanning path.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s yellow utility bar and white navigation header, the page opens with a wide hero band showing the workshop or signage, stretching edge‑to‑edge across the viewport. On the left side of this hero, roughly one quarter in from the left edge, a bold white service title sits over the image, echoing the ‘Services Provided’ treatment from the mockup. As you scroll slightly, a light, semi‑opaque text panel overlaps the lower portion of the hero from the left, occupying just over half the central content width. Inside it, the service name, a short summary, and several paragraphs of explanatory text are laid out with comfortable spacing, followed by a clearly separated package selector and any quote‑only notice. To the right, aligned with the same vertical band, a tall photo of a mechanic working under the hood extends down, visually pairing with the text but keeping the focus on the left. Below this overlapping composition, the background shifts to a plain white or very light gray content area. Here, a prominent but compact availability search form spans most of the centered content width, followed by a slim filter bar, a brief availability summary line, and then a column of availability result cards. Each card stretches across the content column with workshop details on the left and a tidy grid of time slots on the right, stacked downward with consistent gaps. The overall effect is orderly, trustworthy, and practical: strong horizontal bands, clear left‑anchored reading paths, and enough photography to humanize the content without overwhelming the service and booking information.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This non‑visual data loader sits logically at the top of the main service detail area, directly below the global header and above the hero content. It occupies no visible space and does not alter the layout flow. Instead, it wraps and controls the rendering of the hero‑style service header, the overlapping service description panel, the package selector, and the quote‑only indicator. While data is loading, it triggers the loading-and-error-state to occupy the same zones those components would use (for example, a skeleton or spinner centered within the hero and overlapping text panel area), maintaining the same spatial footprint so the overall page structure remains stable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered wrapper in normal state; in loading states it causes an inline placeholder or overlay within the hero and overlapping text panel area, but does not change document flow outside that region.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The service header behaves as the hero content overlay. It appears immediately below the global header/navigation, filling a horizontal band that is roughly 25–30% of the initial viewport height. A full‑width background image of the workshop, signage, or exterior stretches from the left to right edges of the viewport. Within a centered max‑width container, the main service title is aligned to the left, starting around 80–120px from the left edge of the container and vertically positioned in the middle third of the hero. The heading text is large, white, and set directly over the image, possibly with a subtle text shadow or soft overlay behind it for legibility. There is generous space above and below the heading (24–32px) so it reads as a prominent anchor. The lower edge of this hero image is designed so that the subsequent overlapping service-description-section begins around the lower quarter of the hero, giving the appearance that the text panel is sliding up over the image from below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static section in normal document flow, spanning the full viewport width; background image scales responsively with a fixed height range to preserve the hero’s visual impact.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The service description section is a light, semi‑opaque rectangular panel that overlaps the bottom of the hero, anchored to the left side of the centered content container. It occupies roughly 55–60% of the container width and extends downward enough to comfortably contain the main service heading, a short intro line, and several paragraphs of body text. The panel starts overlapping the hero by about 25–30% of its height, creating a layered effect, and continues down until it transitions fully into the plain page background. It has 24–32px of internal padding on all sides and a subtle drop shadow or border to distinguish it from the hero image behind. The top of the panel aligns roughly with the left edge of the hero heading above, reinforcing the left‑anchored reading flow. Within the panel, content is stacked vertically: title at the top, then an optional subheading or short summary, then one or more paragraphs with 16–24px spacing between them. Beneath the paragraphs is reserved vertical space for the service-package-selector and quote-only-indicator, which visually belong to the same panel. On smaller screens, this panel transitions to a full‑width block sitting directly below a shorter hero, losing the lateral overlap but preserving the sense of a featured content box.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlapping block positioned using negative margin or relative/absolute positioning over the lower portion of the hero, but otherwise behaving as part of the normal scrollable page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The package selector is nested inside the lower portion of the service-description-section panel, following the last paragraph of text. It spans nearly the full internal width of the panel, leaving 16–24px padding to the left and right edges. There is a clear vertical gap of about 24px between the final paragraph and the top of the selector, creating a visual break between narrative content and interactive options. The selector itself can be arranged as a small vertical stack or grid of options (for example, radio cards or bordered rows), each option occupying the full width of the panel’s content area. Each option row has 16–24px internal padding and 8–16px vertical spacing between rows. If pricing or duration is shown, they are right‑aligned within each option row to align with the panel’s internal grid, echoing the mockup’s neat, brochure‑like alignment. On mobile, all options remain stacked vertically, with touch‑friendly spacing preserved.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, block-level component contained within the overlapping text panel; expands vertically as options are added without affecting the hero or right‑side image positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote‑only indicator appears within the same left text panel as the service description and package selector, positioned close to the booking‑relevant UI. When active, it sits either directly above the package selector or aligned to the top‑right corner within the selector area, depending on implementation. Visually, it is a compact horizontal row: a small icon followed by concise text (e.g., ‘Quote only for this service’). It uses a high‑contrast accent color from the brand palette (likely the yellow) against the panel’s light background, but is set in a small font size so it reads as an informative status rather than a dominant heading. There is a modest vertical margin (8–16px) separating it from surrounding text or selector controls to keep the layout airy and consistent with the mockup’s spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline/block element within the service-description-section; its presence or absence slightly adjusts vertical spacing but does not alter the broader layout structure.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The availability search form introduces a new section below the overlapping hero and description composition. It begins roughly 32–48px below the bottom of the service-description-section panel, with the background now a clean white or very light gray. The form sits inside the same centered max‑width container as the text panel above, but this time spans a larger share of the width—about 80–90% of the container—so that the inputs feel substantial but not edge‑to‑edge. The form is left‑aligned, with its title or label row at the top (optional), and then a single main row of fields: location, date, and any additional selectors, followed by a prominent ‘Search’ button. On large screens, these inputs form a horizontal row that consumes roughly one third to one half of the viewport width, with 16–24px gaps between fields, and the button anchored to the far right of the row. On smaller screens, the inputs stack vertically with consistent 16px vertical spacing. The entire form block has 24–32px of internal padding and a subtle border or background tint to set it apart from the plain page, echoing the structured, brochure‑like feel of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block in the main document flow; expands vertically on smaller screens as form fields wrap or stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter bar sits directly below the availability-search-form, separated by a modest vertical gap of 16–24px to keep the relationship clear but not cramped. It spans the full width of the central content container and is relatively slim in height. The bar consists of small, horizontally arranged filter controls (e.g., dropdowns or pill buttons for ‘Time of day’, ‘Distance’, ‘Workshop type’) aligned to the left with 8–16px gaps between them. On the far right of the bar, there may be a light ‘Reset filters’ link or icon. The background is either the same as the page or slightly tinted, with a top or bottom border line to read as a control band rather than a large panel. On narrow viewports, filters wrap to a second line, maintaining the left alignment and 8–12px vertical spacing between wrapped rows. The bar visually anchors the top of the results area and echoes the horizontal emphasis seen in the mockup’s navigation and utility bar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full‑width row directly under the search form; may remain visible when results scroll below, but does not need sticky behavior to align with the mockup’s simple, non‑sticky patterns.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This non‑visual data loader resides conceptually beneath the availability-filter-bar and wraps the availability-summary, availability-results-list, and loading-and-error-state components. It itself has no visible UI and no fixed height; instead, it controls which of its child components are rendered based on the search state. For example, immediately after a search submission, it may render the loading-and-error-state placeholder where the results list would normally appear, and when data arrives, it transitions to showing the availability-summary followed by the availability-results-list. Its footprint is therefore exactly the combined height of these child components, keeping the page’s vertical flow consistent.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered wrapper that affects only which child components occupy the results region; does not introduce additional spacing or structural changes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The availability summary appears as a concise line or short block of text immediately above the results list, positioned about 16px below the availability-filter-bar. It spans the full width of the content container but is typically only one or two lines tall. The text is left‑aligned and communicates the current search outcome (for example, ‘8 workshops found near Richmond for 15 Mar’), with optional small icons or emphasis on the number of locations. To maintain the moderate density, there is 8–12px of space above and below this line. On the right side of the same row, there may be a subtle secondary control (such as sorting) if required, sized to not overpower the message. The summary acts as a visual bridge between the controls above and the results below, mirroring the mockup’s use of simple, functional text to guide the user.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline/block element sitting at the top of the results region; its height adjusts to the length of its message but otherwise follows normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results list is a vertical stack of cards occupying the main lower portion of the page beneath the availability summary. It is contained within the same centered content column, spanning nearly its full width (with 24–32px margins to the container edges). Each result item is a rectangular card with a white background, thin border, and 24px internal padding. There is 16–24px vertical space between consecutive cards, keeping the overall density consistent with the mockup’s content blocks. Inside each card, content is arranged in a two‑column layout: the left 60–65% holds the workshop name, address, distance, and key attributes; the right 35–40% is reserved for the availability-result-item-times component, aligned to the top of the card. On smaller screens, this layout collapses into a vertical stack where the right column content (times) moves underneath the left details, still within the same card but with a 16px gap between sections. The list extends downward as many items as needed, creating a scrollable column that feels like a structured brochure listing with clear, repeatable patterns.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically scrolling list; cards expand in height based on content but maintain consistent width and spacing within the central container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Within each result card, the availability-result-item-times component occupies the right‑hand column on larger screens. It is a vertically stacked group of time slot options and related booking actions. The component takes up about 35–40% of the card’s width and aligns flush with the card’s right padding edge. Time slots are represented as pill‑shaped or bordered buttons arranged in one or two rows, with 8–12px horizontal gaps and 8px vertical gaps between them, keeping the layout clean and easy to scan. A label such as ‘Available times’ may appear above the row of buttons, aligned left within this right column. On smaller screens, this entire block shifts below the left column content and expands to full card width, with time pills wrapping naturally across the line. The visual styling is clear and functional, matching the mockup’s emphasis on practicality over trendiness.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sub-component constrained to the right portion of its parent card on wide screens; reflows beneath the main details area on narrow screens while maintaining internal spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading-and-error-state component appears only when data is being fetched or when errors occur, occupying the same spatial regions as the content it replaces. For service details, it may present as a semi‑transparent overlay or skeleton lines within the hero and overlapping text panel, matching their shape and size so the page doesn’t visually jump. For availability search, it appears in the results region under the availability-summary area: a bordered or lightly tinted box spanning the full width of the content container, with a spinner or warning icon centered horizontally and vertically within. The box has 24–32px of internal padding and 16–24px margin above and below, aligning with the surrounding cards’ spacing so it feels integrated into the layout. Error messages are kept concise, in a similar text size as body copy, to maintain the calm, corporate tone of the design.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces hero text/content or results list inline within the normal flow; does not permanently alter layout structure and disappears once content loads successfully.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state sync component is entirely non‑visual and has no dedicated on‑screen footprint. Conceptually, it sits alongside the availability-search-form and availability-filter-bar in the code structure, listening to their values and reflecting them in the URL. It does not reserve any height or width and therefore does not affect spacing or alignment. Users experience its effects only through behaviors like the page reloading with the same filters applied, but visually the layout remains identical whether it is present or not.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible, zero‑dimension state manager; exists outside of the visual layout and does not modify the stacking or spacing of other components.

name: Schedule Service / Book Appointment
title: Schedule Service
description: Page for selecting a location-specific service, choosing a date and time, setting visit options, and booking an automotive service appointment.

core_features: Load the service catalog for the specified locationId using getLocationServiceCatalog and allow users to select a service and service package that are actually available at that location.
Respect optional URL parameters (serviceId, servicePackageId, date) to preselect the service, package, and appointment date when the page loads.
Allow users to choose or change the appointment date, then retrieve available appointment time slots for the selected service package and date via getAppointmentTimeSlots.
Display available appointment time slots (with associated price and timing data) and let users select a specific time slot for their appointment.
Provide visit options for drop-off, wait on-site, or shuttle when supported by the location (using Location.offers_free_shuttle) and collect shuttle pickup address only when relevant.
Allow users to enter additional notes related to their appointment (e.g., issues to check) and include these in the createAppointment call.
Validate that location, service package, date, time slot, and visit type are all selected before submitting a booking.
Create an appointment by calling createAppointment with the selected locationId, servicePackageId, timeSlotId, computed appointmentDatetime, visitType, shuttlePickupAddress, and notes.
Show an in-page appointment confirmation state after a successful booking using data returned by createAppointment (Appointment, Location, ServicePackage, AppointmentTimeSlot, confirmation_message).
Provide navigation to location_detail.html for the current location and to cart_checkout.html via a "Proceed to Checkout" action after the appointment is successfully created.
user_workflows:
Workflow A: Book a preselected service at a chosen location
1. User arrives from service_detail.html with locationId, serviceId, servicePackageId, and optionally date in the URL.
2. Page-state controller reads URL parameters and validates that locationId is present; if missing or invalid, it shows an error state and does not proceed.
3. The page calls getLocationServiceCatalog(locationId).
