4. On successful response, the service selector preselects the service and package matching serviceId and servicePackageId (if still available at this location). If not available, it clears preselection but keeps catalog visible for manual selection.
5. The date picker initializes to the date from the URL if provided; otherwise it uses an application-defined default (e.g., the earliest date the UI allows), without hardcoding a specific calendar date.
6. Once a valid servicePackageId and date are both set, the page calls getAppointmentTimeSlots(locationId, servicePackageId, date).
7. The time slot selector displays the returned slots and the user selects one.
8. The user chooses visit type (drop_off, wait, or shuttle). If shuttle is chosen and the location offers a shuttle, the user enters a shuttle pickup address. If the location does not offer a shuttle, the shuttle option is disabled or shows an informational message.
9. The user optionally enters notes.
10. The review panel shows a summary of service package, price (from the selected time slot), date, time, and visit options.
11. The user clicks "Book Appointment"; the appointment form validates inputs and calls createAppointment with the selected parameters, using the start_datetime of the chosen time slot as appointmentDatetime.
12. On success, the page displays the confirmation panel with details from the createAppointment response and shows a "Proceed to Checkout" navigation action.
13. If the user clicks "Proceed to Checkout", the browser navigates to cart_checkout.html (optionally including URL parameters such as locationId or appointmentId if desired by the broader application).

Workflow B: Start from location page and choose a service on this screen
1. User arrives from location_detail.html or locations.html with only locationId (and optionally date) in the URL.
2. The page-state controller reads locationId and calls getLocationServiceCatalog(locationId).
3. The location summary displays the selected location information.
4. Service catalog is shown grouped by categories; no service is preselected.
5. User browses services and selects a service that is not quote-only. If a selected service has multiple active packages, the user selects one package from the list.
6. The date picker initializes using the URL date if provided; otherwise it uses a default date.
7. After the user chooses a date and a service package, the page calls getAppointmentTimeSlots(locationId, servicePackageId, date).
8. The user selects a desired time slot.
9. The user chooses visit type and optionally provides shuttle pickup address and notes.
10. The review panel updates dynamically as selections change.
11. The user clicks "Book Appointment"; the page validates the selections and calls createAppointment.
12. On success, the confirmation panel shows appointment details and the user can either remain on the page, navigate back to the location detail page, or proceed to checkout.

Workflow C: User changes their mind mid-flow (change service, date, or time)
1. User has already selected a service package, date, and a time slot.
2. The user changes the date in the date picker.
3. The page updates the date parameter in the URL (without a page reload) and invalidates the currently selected time slot.
4. The page calls getAppointmentTimeSlots(locationId, currentServicePackageId, newDate).
5. The time slot selector shows new slots; the user selects a new time.
6. The review panel updates with the new date and time, keeping the chosen visit type and notes unchanged.
7. If the user changes the service or service package instead, the page updates serviceId and/or servicePackageId in the URL and clears the previous time slot selection, then fetches slots using getAppointmentTimeSlots for the new package and current date.
8. When the user is satisfied, they proceed to book using createAppointment as in other workflows.
interactions: On page load, read URL parameters (locationId, serviceId, servicePackageId, date). If locationId is missing or invalid, show a blocking error message with no calls to getLocationServiceCatalog.

On successful retrieval of the service catalog via getLocationServiceCatalog, bind the location data to the location summary component and the catalog structure (categories, services, packages) to the service selector component.

When the user selects a service in the service selector, update in-memory state for serviceId and, if possible, auto-select a default package for that service (where a package is marked is_default and is_available). Also update the URL serviceId parameter.

When the user selects a specific service package, update in-memory state for servicePackageId, update the URL servicePackageId parameter, and, if a date is already chosen, trigger a new call to getAppointmentTimeSlots.

If a selected service is marked is_quote_only, the service selector prevents direct package/time selection and instead shows a non-interactive informational message (without calling any quote-related APIs).

When the user selects or changes a date in the date picker, update the in-memory date state and update the date parameter in the URL. If a servicePackageId is already selected, call getAppointmentTimeSlots with the new date.

Upon initiating a getAppointmentTimeSlots call, the time slot selector enters a loading state, hides or disables any previously loaded slots, and, on success, renders the new list of available AppointmentTimeSlot options.

If getAppointmentTimeSlots returns an empty list or indicates no available slots, the time slot selector shows a "no availability" message and requires the user to pick a different date or service package.

When the user clicks on a time slot in the time slot selector, update selected timeSlotId in state and mark that slot as selected; the review panel updates to show start and end time plus price from that slot.

Visit type selection (drop_off, wait, shuttle) is handled via radio buttons or equivalent controls. Changing visit type updates state.visitType and immediately affects validation and the visibility/requirement of the shuttle pickup address field.

If visitType is set to shuttle and the location.offers_free_shuttle flag is true, the shuttle pickup address input becomes visible and required before booking. If offers_free_shuttle is false, the shuttle option is disabled or produces an informational message without changing state.visitType.
Notes text area accepts free-form user input and updates state.notes; its content is passed through to createAppointment as the notes parameter.

The appointment review panel listens for changes in selected service package, date, time slot, visitType, shuttlePickupAddress, and notes, and re-renders a summary without requiring additional API calls.

Clicking "Book Appointment" triggers synchronous client-side validation (ensuring locationId, servicePackageId, date, timeSlotId, and visitType are all present, plus shuttlePickupAddress if required). If validation fails, the page displays inline errors and does not call createAppointment.

On successful validation, the page calls createAppointment(locationId, servicePackageId, timeSlotId, appointmentDatetime, visitType, shuttlePickupAddress, notes), where appointmentDatetime is derived from the selected time slot's start_datetime.
During the createAppointment call, the appointment form shows a loading/disabled state on the button to prevent duplicate submissions.

If createAppointment succeeds, the page stores the returned appointment object and related entities in in-memory state and displays the confirmation panel. The booking form inputs can either be locked or hidden to prevent edits after confirmation.
If createAppointment fails, the page displays an error message in the booking area, keeps the current selections intact, and allows the user to retry after fixing any issues.
Clicking the location name or explicit "View Location Details" link navigates to location_detail.html with the current locationId as a URL parameter.
Clicking "Proceed to Checkout" from the confirmation panel navigates to cart_checkout.html as specified in the outgoing navigation configuration.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page’s primary source of state is the URL query string combined with data from the assigned interfaces. On initial load, the page reads locationId, serviceId, servicePackageId, and date from the URL. If locationId is present, it calls getLocationServiceCatalog(locationId) and binds the resulting location and service catalog data to components. If serviceId and/or servicePackageId are present, the service selector attempts to preselect matching items from the catalog; if those items are not available at this location or not present in the catalog, the preselection is ignored and the user must manually choose a service. If a valid date is present in the URL, the date picker uses it as the initial value; otherwise, the component initializes with a default date determined by the application without hardcoding a specific calendar date. Whenever the user changes the service selection, package selection, or date, the page updates the corresponding URL parameters (serviceId, servicePackageId, date) using client-side navigation methods (such as history.pushState or equivalent) to keep the URL in sync without full page reloads. When both a valid servicePackageId and a date are set in state, the page calls getAppointmentTimeSlots(locationId, servicePackageId, date); the resulting time slots are held in in-memory state and not serialized to the URL. Selection of a specific time slot is stored in state (timeSlotId) but not required to appear in the URL. Visit type, shuttle pickup address, and notes are also maintained only in in-memory state. After a successful createAppointment call, the appointment object returned is stored in state and used to render the confirmation panel; the application may append optional parameters such as appointmentId or a confirmation flag to the URL for analytics or downstream navigation, but the core confirmation display relies on the in-memory response rather than re-querying. The page does not use localStorage or other persistent client storage; all persistent routing context is via URL parameters, and all transient user choices and API responses live in memory for the duration of the visit.

components.functionality: Initializes and orchestrates page-level state by reading URL parameters, calling getLocationServiceCatalog, coordinating subsequent calls to getAppointmentTimeSlots and createAppointment, and propagating state updates to child components.
Displays the current location’s name, address, and key attributes based on the Location entity, and provides navigation to the full location detail page.

Shows the list of service categories, services, and available service packages for the current location, derived from getLocationServiceCatalog. Supports selecting a non-quote-only service and one of its available service packages, and prevents booking for services flagged as quote-only.
Allows the user to select the appointment date, initialized from the URL date parameter when present, and triggers fetching of time slots when both date and servicePackageId are set.

Displays available appointment time slots for the selected date and service package using data from getAppointmentTimeSlots and lets the user choose one slot. Handles loading, empty, and error states.
Captures visit type (drop_off, wait, shuttle) and shuttle pickup address when applicable based on Location.offers_free_shuttle, along with free-form appointment notes.

Shows a live summary of the pending appointment, including selected service package, price derived from the chosen AppointmentTimeSlot, date, time, visit type, shuttle address, and notes, to allow the user to review before booking.
Hosts the primary booking action button (e.g., "Book Appointment"), performs client-side validation of required fields, and initiates the createAppointment call via the state controller.

Displays confirmation details after a successful createAppointment call using the returned Appointment, Location, ServicePackage, and optionally AppointmentTimeSlot entities, along with the confirmation_message. Provides actions to proceed to checkout or view location details.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow with a clear hierarchy: utility bar and main header (outside this spec), then a full-width hero with a workshop/building photo and a left-aligned page title like “Schedule Service”. Directly beneath, an overlapping two-column booking area mirrors the mockup’s text/image composition: the left column holds the step-by-step booking controls, and the right column holds the appointment review/summary. Confirmation content reuses the same central container but simplifies into a single dominant panel after submission.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow with a clear hierarchy: utility bar and main header (outside this spec), then a full-width hero with a workshop/building photo and a left-aligned page title like “Schedule Service”. Directly beneath, an overlapping two-column booking area mirrors the mockup’s text/image composition: the left column holds the step-by-step booking controls, and the right column holds the appointment review/summary. Confirmation content reuses the same central container but simplifies into a single dominant panel after submission.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The combined header (utility bar + main nav) occupies ~10–15% of the viewport height, the hero image band ~25–30%, and the overlapping booking area fills the remaining first-screen height. The main content sits in a centered max-width container (~1120–1200px) with generous left-right margins (~80–120px at large breakpoints). Inside the booking area, the left functional column uses about 60–65% of the width (roughly 7 of 12 columns), and the right summary column uses 35–40% (about 5 of 12). Vertical gaps between major groups (location summary, service selection, date/time, options, review) follow the 32–48px section spacing from the mockup; padding inside each card/module is 16–24px on all sides.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density matches the brochure-like, moderately spacious feel. Form sections are grouped logically with clear headings and short helper text, separated by comfortable white space. Fields are not crammed: each block breathes with 16–24px padding and 16–24px vertical spacing between control groups. The right column limits itself to a compact but readable stack of summary information rather than long text. Hero photography and subtle background imagery balance the functional density to avoid a purely utilitarian form wall.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow follows an F-pattern: the eye first hits the top-left logo/nav, then scans across the top bars, then down to the large left-aligned hero title (“Schedule Service”). From there, attention drops to the overlapping booking card where the location summary sits at the top-left, followed by service selection and date/time controls flowing downward. The right-hand review panel acts like the mechanic photo in the mockup—secondary but strong—drawing the eye rightward after users understand the main steps. A full-width, brand-colored submit bar at the bottom reinforces the final call-to-action and caps the F-pattern with a horizontal emphasis across the page.

layout.overall_description: Within the existing site chrome (yellow utility bar and white navigation header), the Schedule Service page opens with a full-width hero photo of the workshop or building signage, slightly dimmed to make a bold white page title stand out on the left side: “Schedule Service” with a smaller line indicating the current location. Overlapping the bottom edge of this hero, a large white booking card sits centered on the page, framed by generous side margins. This card is split into a two-column layout: a wider left column for interactive booking steps and a narrower right column for a running appointment summary. The left column stacks the location summary, service package selector, date and time selection, and visit options into clearly separated bands, each with modest padding and clean labels. The right column reads like a tidy sidebar, visually tied to the automotive imagery via subtle color blocks or a faint workshop photo accent. Below the booking card, a full-width yellow submit bar spans edge-to-edge, containing the total price and a prominent confirmation button, sometimes sticking to the viewport bottom for easy access. After submission, the main form content collapses into a centered confirmation panel that occupies most of the content width, while the rest of the page background (hero imagery, header) remains consistent, preserving the overall layered, trustworthy, small-business feel of the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component does not render as a visible UI element. It conceptually sits behind the entire content area, spanning the full width and height of the page content region and orchestrating which of the booking states (form vs. confirmation) is visible. It does not consume any dedicated visual space; instead, it controls visibility and enabled/disabled states of the other components while preserving their spatial positions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, purely logical. It does not affect layout directly other than toggling the presence/absence of visible components in their defined positions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location summary appears near the top-left of the overlapping booking card, immediately below the hero band where the page title sits. It forms a full-width strip within the left column of the card, but visually feels like a compact header module: a horizontal row with a location name on the left and subtle icons (pin, phone) on the right. On large screens, it occupies the uppermost 10–15% of the left column’s height, with 16–24px padding around the text. The top edge of this strip aligns roughly with the bottom of the hero image, creating the sense that the booking card is sliding up underneath the hero. On smaller screens, it is still the first block inside the main booking card, spanning 100% of the card width above the other form controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the booking card; it stays anchored at the top of the left column as the user scrolls the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The service-package selector is directly below the location summary in the left column of the booking card. It spans the full width of that left column (about 60–65% of the card width on desktop) and reads as a prominent white panel with a clear heading like “Choose Your Service”. This component uses the mockup’s moderate density: a bold label at the top, then a vertical stack of labeled service options or cards, each separated by 8–16px. The top margin between the location summary and this selector is around 24–32px, using the section margin rhythm. On smaller screens, this component stretches to 100% of the card width and appears directly beneath the location summary, maintaining similar padding and vertical spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls with the rest of the content in the left column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The date picker sits beneath the service-package selector and shares a horizontal row with the timeslot selector, echoing the mockup’s two-column content split. Within the left column of the booking card, this row is divided into two sub-columns: the date picker on the left occupying roughly 40–45% of the row width, and the timeslot grid on the right occupying 55–60%. The date picker’s container has 16–24px of internal padding and a clear label like “Select Date” at the top, with the calendar control filling the rest. The top margin above this row matches the section spacing (24–32px) so it clearly reads as the next step after choosing a service. On mobile and narrow tablets, the date picker stacks above the timeslot selector, each taking 100% width of the card with 16–24px vertical separation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within its row; participates in a responsive two-column-to-single-column behavior with the timeslot-selector.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The timeslot selector is positioned immediately to the right of the date-picker-control within the same horizontal row inside the left booking column. It occupies about 55–60% of that row’s width on large screens, aligning its top edge with the date picker’s top so the two components feel like a paired step. Internally, it presents a grid of available time blocks with 8–16px gaps between rows and columns, all contained within a white panel with 16–24px padding. The grid does not stretch edge-to-edge of the entire page; it is constrained to the left column to maintain the two-column card structure. On small screens, this selector stacks beneath the date picker, using full card width and the same vertical spacing system to maintain clarity.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the booking workflow; responsive such that it shifts from being right-aligned next to the date picker to full-width stacked beneath it on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The visit-options form sits directly beneath the date-and-time row in the left column, spanning the full width of that column. It is separated from the row above by a 24–32px vertical gap, following the mockup’s section rhythm. The form appears as a grouped set of controls (e.g., drop-off vs. wait, loan car, notes) arranged in a vertical sequence with 8–16px spacing between fields. The container has 16–24px padding, with a slightly smaller heading compared to the main page title but consistent with other section headings. On desktop, the bottom edge of this form aligns roughly with or slightly below the bottom of the right-hand appointment review panel, so the two columns feel balanced in height. On smaller screens, it becomes another full-width block following the stacked date and time components, maintaining the same internal spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static; it expands vertically as options are added but remains within the left side of the overlapping booking card.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment review panel occupies the entire right column of the overlapping booking card, mirroring the mechanic-photo area in the mockup. On large screens, it begins at the top edge of the booking card, horizontally aligned with the top of the location summary and service selector in the left column. It uses about 35–40% of the card width and is flush to the card’s right edge, with 16–24px padding inside. The panel is vertically segmented: summary header at the top, followed by grouped details (location, selected service, date/time, visit options, estimated total) with 8–16px spacing. Behind or above the content, you can optionally include a subtle workshop or mechanic photo accent, occupying the upper 30–40% of this panel and fading into a solid background at the bottom, echoing the mockup’s right-side imagery while keeping text legible. On smaller screens, this review panel moves below the entire left-column flow, spanning the full card width with extra top margin (~32px) so it still feels distinct but secondary to the form steps.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On taller screens, it may use a sticky-within-column behavior: staying visible as the user scrolls through the left-column steps until the bottom of the booking card is reached. Otherwise it scrolls normally, always remaining in the right column on desktop and as a stacked block on mobile.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment submit bar is a full-width horizontal band that appears immediately below the main overlapping booking card, spanning the entire content width from left margin to right margin. It echoes the bright yellow utility bar at the top of the site, creating a strong horizontal anchor near the bottom of the viewport. The bar is relatively slim in height (about 56–72px), with 16–24px vertical padding and content arranged in a left-right split: a concise summary (e.g., selected date and estimated total) on the left, and a prominent blue primary button (“Confirm Booking”) on the right. At larger viewports, the bar visually tucks just under the booking card, with about 24px of vertical separation. On mobile, it still spans edge-to-edge and remains easily tappable, sometimes appearing visually closer to the bottom of the screen than to other content blocks.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky to the bottom of the viewport once the booking card enters view: as the user scrolls through the form, this bar pins to the bottom edge of the screen so the call-to-action and price are always available. It un-sticks when scrolled back above the booking section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: After a successful submission, the appointment confirmation panel replaces the active booking area as the main focus within the content container. It appears centered on the page, roughly where the overlapping booking card was, using a single-column card layout that spans about 60–70% of the content width on desktop (and nearly 100% on mobile). The hero image and global header remain visible above, but the two-column booking card disappears or is visually collapsed, so the confirmation panel becomes the dominant white card overlapping the lower part of the hero. Inside, content is organized with a bold confirmation heading at the top, a short paragraph of reassurance, and then a structured summary of appointment details laid out in a clean, vertical list with 8–16px spacing. There is comfortable white space (24–32px padding) around all sides, with optional secondary actions (e.g., ‘Add to calendar’) aligned to the right or bottom. The overall proportions echo the earlier booking card but simplified and more centered, conveying completion and clarity.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Mutually exclusive with the active booking state: when this panel is visible, the main booking form components and submit bar are hidden. It is static, scrolling naturally if content exceeds the viewport height, while remaining anchored within the same max-width, centered container as the rest of the page.

name: Coupons & Deals
title: Coupons & Deals
description: Browse current coupons and promotional offers, filter them by category and minimum discount, and navigate to coupon details or eligible services.

core_features: Read optional URL parameters (category, minDiscountPercent, locationId) on load and initialize the page state accordingly.
Call the listCoupons interface with the current category, minDiscountPercent, and locationId state to retrieve matching coupons.
Display the returned coupons in a structured list, showing each coupon’s key details (name, discount_label, category, description, valid_to).
Provide filter controls for coupon category and minimum discount percentage that update the URL parameters and re-fetch coupons via listCoupons.
Respect an optional locationId context by passing it through to listCoupons and indicating that results may be location-specific.
Allow users to click a coupon entry to navigate to coupon_detail.html with the selected couponId as a URL parameter.
Allow users to click a “View eligible services” action on a coupon entry to navigate to services.html with the coupon’s category as a URL parameter.
Handle cases where no coupons are returned by showing a functional empty state and allowing users to adjust filters.
user_workflows:
Workflow: View coupons from general navigation
1. User opens coupons.html without URL parameters (via header or footer navigation).
2. coupons-page-controller reads the URL (no category, minDiscountPercent, or locationId) and initializes an unfiltered state.
3. coupons-page-controller calls listCoupons with undefined/null parameters for category, minDiscountPercent, and locationId.
4. listCoupons returns an array of coupon objects; coupon-list displays them using coupon-tile components.
5. User scrolls the list and optionally clicks a coupon-tile to open coupon_detail.html?couponId={coupon_id} in the same tab or a new tab, depending on implementation.

Workflow: Arrive with a pre-selected category from services.html
1. User clicks a link or button on services.html that points to coupons.html?category={categorySlug}.
2. coupons-page-controller parses the category parameter and sets internal state.category to the provided value.
3. coupons-page-controller calls listCoupons with category set, minDiscountPercent and locationId taken from URL if present or left undefined.
4. coupon-filter-bar initializes its UI to show the active category filter.
5. coupon-list shows only coupons for that category.
6. User may clear the category filter (e.g., by selecting a neutral option) which triggers a URL update (category parameter removed) and a new listCoupons call.

Workflow: Arrive scoped to a shop from location_detail.html
1. User clicks a link on location_detail.html that points to coupons.html?locationId={locationId} (optionally with category).
2. coupons-page-controller reads locationId and passes it to listCoupons along with any category or minDiscountPercent from the URL.
3. coupon-results-summary indicates that coupons are scoped to a specific location context (without needing location details, since only listCoupons is available).
4. User reviews the list; all results are implicitly constrained by the locationId passed to listCoupons.
5. User can change category or minDiscountPercent; coupons-page-controller keeps locationId fixed in the URL and subsequent listCoupons calls until the user clears it (e.g., by using a “clear location filter” action that removes locationId from the URL and triggers a re-fetch).

Workflow: Filter by minimum discount and explore services
1. User is on coupons.html with a list of coupons already displayed.
2. In coupon-filter-bar, the user adjusts the minimum discount input (e.g., from a lower value to a higher value).
3. coupon-filter-bar triggers onChangeMinDiscount, which updates the minDiscountPercent parameter in the URL (or adds it if it was missing) and notifies coupons-page-controller.
4. coupons-page-controller calls listCoupons with the new minDiscountPercent while preserving category and locationId state.
5. coupon-list updates to show only coupons meeting the new minimum discount.
6. User finds a coupon of interest and clicks “View eligible services” in that coupon-tile.
7. The page navigates to services.html?category={coupon.category}, allowing the user to see services that generally match the coupon’s category.
interactions:
Interaction: Initial page load
- Trigger: Browser loads coupons.html.
- Behavior: coupons-page-controller reads URL query parameters (category, minDiscountPercent, locationId), normalizes them, and calls listCoupons with these values. The returned coupon array is stored in page state and passed to coupon-list, coupon-results-summary, and related components.

Interaction: Change category filter
- Trigger: User selects a different category option in coupon-filter-bar (or clears the category).
- Behavior: coupon-filter-bar fires onChangeCategory with the selected category value (or null/undefined when cleared). coupons-page-controller updates its internal state.category, modifies the URL query string (adding/updating/removing the category parameter), and calls listCoupons again with the updated category while preserving minDiscountPercent and locationId. coupon-list re-renders using the new results.

Interaction: Change minimum discount filter
- Trigger: User modifies the minimum discount control (e.g., numeric input) in coupon-filter-bar.
- Behavior: coupon-filter-bar fires onChangeMinDiscount with the new numeric value. coupons-page-controller updates state.minDiscountPercent, synchronizes the minDiscountPercent parameter in the URL, and calls listCoupons with the new value (plus existing category and locationId). coupon-list updates with new results.

Interaction: Clear all filters
- Trigger: User clicks a “Clear filters” control in coupon-filter-bar.
- Behavior: coupon-filter-bar fires onClearFilters. coupons-page-controller resets state.category and state.minDiscountPercent (and optionally state.locationId, if business rules permit clearing location context), updates the URL to remove these parameters, and calls listCoupons with undefined/null filter parameters. coupon-list shows the unfiltered (or location-only-filtered) result set.

Interaction: Click a coupon to view details
- Trigger: User clicks the main body or a “View details” control on a coupon-tile.
- Behavior: coupon-tile fires onClickCoupon with the coupon_id. coupons-page-controller (or direct link from coupon-tile) navigates the browser to coupon_detail.html?couponId={coupon_id}.

Interaction: View eligible services for a coupon
- Trigger: User clicks a “View eligible services” action within a coupon-tile.
- Behavior: coupon-tile fires onClickViewServices with the coupon.category (selectedCouponCategory). The browser navigates to services.html?category={coupon.category}.

Interaction: Retry after empty or error state
- Trigger: User clicks a “Retry” or “Adjust filters” control when coupon-list shows no results or an error loading coupons.
- Behavior: For “Retry”, coupons-page-controller re-calls listCoupons with the current filter parameters. For “Adjust filters”, focus may be moved to coupon-filter-bar where the user can modify category and minDiscountPercent, which updates the URL and triggers a new listCoupons call.

Interaction: Browser back/forward navigation
- Trigger: User uses browser back/forward buttons after changing filters.
- Behavior: When the URL changes due to navigation, coupons-page-controller listens for URL changes, re-parses the category, minDiscountPercent, and locationId parameters, updates internal state, and re-calls listCoupons with the new parameters so the visible coupon list matches the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: On initial load, the page parses the query parameters category, minDiscountPercent, and locationId from the coupons.html URL. These values become the canonical state for the page. The coupons-page-controller uses that state to call listCoupons({ category, minDiscountPercent, locationId }). The results from listCoupons are considered the single source of truth for what coupons should be displayed. Whenever the user interacts with filter controls, the page updates the URL query string to reflect the new state (e.g., changing category updates or removes the category parameter; changing the minimum discount updates minDiscountPercent). After each change, coupons-page-controller re-calls listCoupons with the current state derived from the URL parameters, ensuring that the displayed coupons always match the URL. The locationId parameter is treated as contextual; if it is present in the URL, it is always passed to listCoupons until explicitly removed from the URL through a user action. Browser back/forward navigation is handled by re-reading the URL parameters whenever they change and re-invoking listCoupons. No state is persisted in localStorage or other client-side storage; the URL parameters are the only persistent state mechanism across page reloads and navigation.

components.functionality: Orchestrates the Coupons & Deals page: reads URL parameters, manages internal filter state, calls listCoupons, and distributes the resulting data to child components.

Provides user controls to filter coupons by category and minimum discount percent, and optionally clear all filters. It reflects the current filter state based on URL parameters provided by coupons-page-controller.
Displays a summary of the current results and context, such as the number of coupons returned from listCoupons and whether filters like category, minDiscountPercent, or locationId are active.
Renders a list of coupons returned by listCoupons as a sequence of coupon-tile components and manages empty/error states.
Represents a single coupon entry, showing name, discount_label, category, description, and valid_to, and exposing actions to view coupon details or view eligible services.
Displays when listCoupons returns an empty array, informing the user that no coupons match the current filters and offering a way to adjust filters or retry.
Indicates when a listCoupons request is in progress to prevent duplicate submissions and provide feedback during loading.
Displays error information if the listCoupons call fails and allows the user to retry loading coupons.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same linear, top-to-bottom sequence as the mockup: global utility bar and header (logo, nav, social) at the very top, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/building photo and a bold white 'Coupons & Deals' heading anchored on the left. Directly beneath, the main coupons content forms an overlapping two-column section: the left column contains the filter controls and results summary within a light, semi-transparent panel that overlaps the lower portion of the hero image; the right column is a taller content area dedicated to the coupon list and its various states (loading, empty, error, tiles). On narrower viewports, this two-column layout collapses into a stacked layout, with filters and summary above the list.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same linear, top-to-bottom sequence as the mockup: global utility bar and header (logo, nav, social) at the very top, then a full-width hero banner with a workshop/building photo and a bold white 'Coupons & Deals' heading anchored on the left. Directly beneath, the main coupons content forms an overlapping two-column section: the left column contains the filter controls and results summary within a light, semi-transparent panel that overlaps the lower portion of the hero image; the right column is a taller content area dedicated to the coupon list and its various states (loading, empty, error, tiles). On narrower viewports, this two-column layout collapses into a stacked layout, with filters and summary above the list.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The utility bar and white header consume roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport, matching the original design. The hero image occupies around 25–30% of the initial viewport height, stretching edge to edge horizontally. The overlapping coupons section fills the remaining initial viewport height and then scrolls vertically as needed. Within the main content container, a centered 12-column grid is used with generous lateral margins (approximately 80–120px on large screens). The left column for filters and summary consumes about 4–5 of 12 columns (~35–40% width), while the right column with the coupon list uses the remaining 7–8 columns (~60–65% width). Vertical spacing between the hero and the overlapping content section is minimized by letting the top of the filters panel sit 24–32px higher, visually overlapping the hero, reducing dead space.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains moderate: filter controls are grouped and clearly spaced with comfortable padding so they feel approachable rather than technical. The results summary is concise and placed close to the filters. The coupon list uses vertically stacked tiles with 16–24px gaps, each tile having sufficient padding, line height, and clear separation of discount, title, and action. There are no tightly packed multi-column grids of coupons; instead, a single column of broad, brochure-like tiles aligns with the clear, practical feel. States like empty, loading, and error use generous whitespace and centered messaging to avoid visual clutter.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F-pattern similar to the mockup. The eye starts at the top-left (logo) and moves across the yellow utility bar and central navigation, then drops to the 'Coupons & Deals' hero headline on the left side of the banner. From there, the eye moves down into the overlapping left column, first hitting the filter bar (primary interaction) and then the results summary just beneath. After orienting with the filters, the eye naturally moves right into the coupon list area and then down through the stack of coupon tiles. Within each tile, the eye moves left-to-right: headline and details on the left, bold discount emphasis and the call-to-action on the right. Strong blue and yellow accents around discounts and buttons support this F-pattern while preserving the straightforward, trustworthy feel.

layout.overall_description: Below the familiar yellow utility bar and white header, a wide hero photograph of the workshop spans the full width of the window. On the left-middle of this hero, a large white heading reading 'Coupons & Deals' sits over the image, echoing the 'Services Provided' treatment from the mockup. The main coupons section appears as a layered block that slightly intrudes into the bottom of the hero, using a subtle overlap. This main section is centered within a max-width container, divided into two columns on larger screens. On the left, a light, semi-transparent panel holds the coupon filters at the top and a concise results summary beneath, with 16–24px padding and rounded corners, giving it the feel of a control hub. On the right, aligned to the same top baseline, runs a taller panel area where coupon tiles stack in a single column, each tile spanning most of that column’s width with generous padding and a clear hierarchy of discount, title, description, and action button. If there are no coupons, a calm empty-state message appears centered within this right column; if loading or in error, that same area switches to a clear loading indicator or error panel. Vertical rhythm is consistent: distinct sections are separated by 24–32px gaps, text is comfortably spaced, and strong blue and yellow accents punctuate key interactive elements. The overall impression is of a functional, slightly traditional automotive promotions page that feels trustworthy, easy to scan, and aligned with the workshop-centric branding.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupons-page-controller occupies the main content region directly beneath the full-width hero banner. It is constrained to a centered max-width container that follows the implicit 12-column grid, with left and right margins of roughly 80–120px on large screens. The top edge of this controller is pulled upward with a slight negative margin so that its top-left panel overlaps the lower portion of the hero by about 24–32px, mirroring the overlapping text panel seen in the mockup. Inside, it establishes a two-column layout: on desktop, the left column spans approximately 4–5 grid columns and holds the coupon-filter-bar followed by the coupon-results-summary stacked vertically; the right column spans about 7–8 grid columns and holds the coupon-list area, which will in turn show tiles, empty state, loading, or error. Vertical spacing inside this controller follows the 8px scale: 24px gap between the hero overlap edge and internal content padding, 16–24px gaps between stacked components. On tablet, the two columns narrow but stay side by side until a breakpoint where they stack vertically: filters and summary appear full-width on top, and the coupon-list sits directly below, with 24–32px vertical separation to keep the hierarchy clear.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard document flow with a slight negative top margin to overlap the hero section; it scrolls normally with the page and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-filter-bar is the topmost element inside the left column of the coupons-page-controller. It appears as a rectangular, light-colored panel (white or very light neutral) with a slight transparency if placed directly over the hero overlap area, and with a soft shadow or subtle border to differentiate it from the workshop background. On large screens, this panel spans the full width of the left column (approximately 35–40% of the content width) and is aligned with the left grid edge, sitting about 24px below the lower edge of the hero heading for clear separation. Inside, filter controls (dropdowns, checkboxes, range sliders) align vertically with 8–16px spacing between them, ensuring the panel feels organized but not cramped. The panel’s padding is 16–24px on all sides, making the controls easy to tap and visually balanced. Horizontally, labels and inputs align left, keeping the reading flow consistent. On smaller screens, this filter bar becomes a full-width block sitting above the coupon-list, maintaining the same padding but increasing its vertical height as controls stack. The bottom of the panel is separated from the coupon-results-summary by a 16px gap, visually connecting them as a unit while keeping them distinct.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a static block within the left column, scrolling naturally with the page; no sticky behavior, but its overlapping position relative to the hero gives it a slightly elevated visual presence.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-results-summary sits directly beneath the coupon-filter-bar within the same left column, maintaining the same horizontal alignment and width (about 35–40% of the main content area on desktop). It is separated from the filter bar by roughly 16px of vertical space. The summary appears as a slimmer information strip or small card: it may share the same light background as the filter panel or use a slightly different shade for subtle differentiation. Padding is modest—around 12–16px vertically and 16–24px horizontally. The main summary text (e.g., '12 coupons found • Minimum 10% off') is left-aligned, with key numbers and discount percentages highlighted in the brand blue or yellow for quick scanning. On tall screens, the total height of the filter + summary stack closely matches or slightly undercuts the visual height of the first few coupon tiles on the right, sustaining the balanced two-column feel of the mockup. On mobile, this summary appears directly under the filter bar, full width, with the same spacing and a clear separation from the coupon-list below (about 24px).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, stacked directly under the filter bar within the left column; it scrolls with the rest of the content and has no floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-list occupies the entire right column inside the coupons-page-controller. On large screens, it begins near the same vertical baseline as the top of the coupon-filter-bar, creating a visually aligned top edge between the two columns, with a horizontal gap of about 32–40px between the left and right columns. The list spans roughly 60–65% of the available content width and extends downward as needed. Within this area, coupon tiles are stacked in a single column, each tile taking the full width of this column minus internal paddings. Vertical spacing between tiles is around 16–24px, creating a clear rhythm and avoiding a crowded feel. If there are many coupons, the list simply extends below the fold, preserving the clean, brochure-like vertical flow. When the empty state, loading indicator, or error panel is active, they are centered or aligned within this same right-column footprint, effectively replacing or temporarily occluding the tiles while preserving the two-column structure of the overall layout. On tablet, the list’s width reduces but remains aligned to the right until the breakpoint where columns stack, at which point the coupon-list becomes a full-width block directly under the filters and summary, still presenting a single vertical column of tiles.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, scrolling vertically with the page; its contents (tiles, loading, empty, error) swap within the same spatial container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each coupon-tile is a horizontal card within the coupon-list, spanning nearly the full width of the right column. The tile uses a flat, light background with a clear border or subtle shadow, and 16–24px padding on all sides, reflecting the straightforward panel style in the mockup. Internally, the tile can be thought of as a mini two-column layout: the left two-thirds of the tile hold the coupon title, a short description, terms, and possibly validity dates, all left-aligned and arranged from top to bottom with 4–8px spacing between lines. The right one-third is reserved for a strong focal point: a large discount value (e.g., '20% OFF') in bold typography with brand blue and yellow accents, and a primary action button (e.g., 'View Details') anchored near the bottom-right corner of the tile. This creates a clear left-to-right reading flow—information first, then discount emphasis and action—mirroring the mockup’s balance of text and strong visual elements. Tile height is flexible but generally falls in the 120–180px range on desktop, depending on content length, maintaining a moderate density. Tiles are separated vertically by 16–24px of whitespace, preventing them from visually merging into each other. On smaller screens, internal alignment remains the same, but the right-side discount and button may stack beneath the title block while still using bold styling and centered or right alignment to keep them visually prominent.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contained entirely within the coupon-list’s flow, each tile is a block-level element in the vertical stack with no special sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-empty-state appears only when the current filters yield no coupons. It occupies the same spatial footprint as the coupon-list within the right column, effectively replacing the standard stack of coupon tiles. The empty-state content is centered horizontally and positioned around the vertical center of the visible portion of the right column’s area when possible, providing a calm, uncluttered message. A simple icon or illustration (if used) sits at the top of the empty-state block, with a short headline (e.g., 'No coupons match your filters') and a brief explanatory line below, all center-aligned. There is generous padding—32–48px—around this content so it feels like a self-contained, airy card within the broader right column. Optionally, a secondary button or link (e.g., 'Clear filters') sits beneath the text with about 16px vertical spacing, encouraging action. The left column filter bar and results summary remain visible and unchanged to maintain the two-column layout and hint to users where they can adjust their search.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Displays in place of the coupon tiles within the coupon-list’s region and scrolls normally; it does not overlay the filters or extend beyond the bounds of the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-loading-indicator is shown within the bounds of the coupon-list area whenever the coupons are being fetched or filters are being applied. On initial load or when a change is significant, the primary loading state appears centered in the right column: a simple spinner or automotive-themed loader sits in the middle of a lightly dimmed background that covers the area where tiles would normally appear, while the left column (filters and summary) remains visible and interactive or temporarily disabled, depending on behavior. The vertical space occupied matches the visible height of the coupon-list region, with at least 32px padding above and below the indicator to align with the page’s spacious feel. In lighter-weight loading scenarios (e.g., small filter tweaks), the indicator may reduce to a thin progress bar at the top edge of the coupon-list area, stretching nearly the full width of the right column but with 8–16px margins from the container edges. This maintains a clear, unobtrusive sense of activity while preserving the underlying layout. In both cases, loading visuals align with the brand colors but remain simple and functional.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as a temporary overlay or replacement within the coupon-list container; it scrolls with the page and does not affect the position of elements in the left column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-error-panel appears in the right column when coupon data cannot be loaded. It occupies the same position and width as the coupon-list, effectively replacing tiles or the loading indicator. The panel is a prominent horizontal card with a light background and a stronger border or accent line along the left edge in an error color (muted red), still respecting the modest, corporate visual style. Padding is 16–24px on all sides. At the top-left of the panel is an error icon or symbol, followed by a concise, bold headline like 'There was a problem loading coupons.' Beneath this sits a short explanatory sentence in smaller text, and a primary or secondary button (e.g., 'Retry') aligned left or right at the bottom of the panel, with about 16px spacing from the text. The panel aligns vertically so its top edge is close to the top of the coupon-list area, maintaining alignment with the top of the filter column and keeping the critical message immediately visible without scrolling. The left column remains visible and unchanged, reinforcing the two-column structure and making it clear the issue is with loading coupons, not the entire page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, replacing the coupon tiles area during an error state; it scrolls like any other block element without overlaying the filters.

name: Coupon Detail & Apply
title: Coupon Detail
description: Displays a specific coupon’s details and lets users apply it to eligible service packages and add them to the cart with the discount applied.

core_features: Load and display full coupon details (name, code, discount type and value, validity, and terms) based on the couponId URL parameter using getCouponDetail.
List services that are eligible for the coupon and allow the user to filter eligible packages by service using data from getCouponDetail.applicable_services and getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon.

List eligible service packages for the selected coupon (and optionally selected service), including before-discount price, estimated discount amount, and estimated price after discount using getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon.
Allow the user to select a specific service package (and quantity) and submit an “Add to Cart with Coupon” action that calls addServiceWithCouponToCart.
Show confirmation and feedback after a successful add-to-cart operation using the response from addServiceWithCouponToCart, including the discounted item summary and updated cart totals.
Provide direct navigation to cart_checkout.html after adding a discounted service (e.g., via “View Cart” or “Proceed to Checkout” controls).
Provide navigation to services.html filtered by the coupon’s category, using a URL parameter category derived from the coupon’s category field.
user_workflows: View coupon details and eligible services: 1) User lands on coupon_detail.html with a couponId in the URL (e.g., coupon_detail.html?couponId={id}). 2) On page load, the page calls getCouponDetail(couponId). 3) The coupon’s core data and terms are displayed. 4) The list of applicable services is shown, allowing the user to see which service types can use this coupon. 5) If the coupon is not active or outside its valid date range, the page shows a non-interactive message and disables the apply/add-to-cart controls.

Filter eligible packages by service: 1) After coupon details load, the page optionally reads a serviceId (or similar) from the URL if present to pre-filter packages. 2) The services selector is populated from getCouponDetail.applicable_services. 3) When the user chooses a service from the selector, the page updates the URL query parameter for the selected service (e.g., coupon_detail.html?couponId={id}&serviceId={serviceId}). 4) The page calls getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon(couponId, serviceId) with the selected serviceId (or with no serviceId to fetch all eligible packages). 5) The returned list of packages updates on-screen.

Select a service package and prepare to add to cart: 1) After eligible packages are loaded, the user selects one package from the list (e.g., via radio button or single-select control). 2) When the user selects a package, the selection is reflected in the URL as a servicePackageId query parameter (e.g., coupon_detail.html?couponId={id}&serviceId={serviceId}&servicePackageId={pkgId}). 3) The selected package’s pricing summary (before discount, estimated discount, estimated after-discount price) is shown in the add-to-cart section. 4) The user sets the desired quantity if quantity is configurable (default can be derived in UI logic but is not hardcoded as data).

Add a discounted service package to the cart: 1) With a coupon and service package selected, the user submits the add-to-cart form (e.g., clicks “Add to Cart with Coupon”). 2) The form calls addServiceWithCouponToCart(servicePackageId, couponId, quantity). 3) While waiting for the response, the form shows a pending state and prevents duplicate submissions. 4) If the call returns success = true, the page displays confirmation using the returned cart and added_item (including any discount_amount and total_price from the CartItem). 5) If success = false, the page shows the returned message as an error state and keeps the selection so the user can try again or adjust choices.

Proceed to cart/checkout or keep browsing services: 1) After a successful add-to-cart operation, the page shows clear calls to action: a link/button to navigate to cart_checkout.html and a link/button to continue browsing services. 2) Clicking the cart/checkout action navigates directly to cart_checkout.html (no additional parameters are required). 3) Clicking the “Browse more services in this category” action navigates to services.html with a category query parameter set to the coupon’s category (e.g., services.html?category={coupon.category}).
interactions: Page load interaction: Reading couponId (and optional serviceId and servicePackageId) from the URL, triggering an initial call to getCouponDetail and then getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon.

Service filter interaction: Changing the selected service in a dropdown or selector; this updates the URL (adding or replacing the serviceId parameter) and triggers a new call to getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon.

Package selection interaction: Selecting a service package from the eligible packages list; this updates a selected package state and writes servicePackageId into the URL, enabling the add-to-cart form for that package.
Quantity adjustment interaction: Changing the quantity input in the add-to-cart form; this updates a local form model used when calling addServiceWithCouponToCart.
Add-to-cart interaction: Submitting the add-to-cart form; this calls addServiceWithCouponToCart and then displays success or error feedback using the returned data.
Navigation to cart: Clicking a button or link after a successful add-to-cart operation to navigate to cart_checkout.html.
Navigation to services: Clicking a link to browse more services in the coupon’s category, navigating to services.html with a category query parameter derived from the coupon’s category field.
Error handling interaction: If API calls fail or return invalid coupon or no eligible packages, the user sees contextual error or informational messages and disabled controls where appropriate.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is controlled through URL query parameters and API responses, without using localStorage. Required URL parameter: couponId (string). On initial load, the page reads couponId from the URL. If couponId is missing or invalid, the page shows an error state and does not call the other interfaces. getCouponDetail(couponId) is always called first. Optional URL parameter: serviceId (string). If present, it is used as the filter parameter when calling getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon(couponId, serviceId); if absent, getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon is called with couponId and a null/undefined serviceId to fetch all eligible packages. Optional URL parameter: servicePackageId (string). If present and the loaded eligible packages contain a matching package, that package is preselected in the eligible packages list and in the add-to-cart form. If no matching package is found, the page ignores the parameter and requires a manual selection. Optional URL parameter: quantity (number). If present and valid, it can be used to prepopulate the quantity field in the add-to-cart form. After key user interactions (changing selected service, changing selected package), the page updates the URL with the corresponding query parameters so the state can be restored on refresh or when the URL is shared. API responses are used to derive UI state: from getCouponDetail, the page determines whether the coupon is active and within its validity window (using is_active, valid_from, valid_to) and conditionally enables the apply/add-to-cart flows. From getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon, the page determines whether any packages are available; if none, the page disables the add-to-cart form and shows a message. From addServiceWithCouponToCart, the page uses success, cart, added_item, and message to show confirmation, update on-page cart indicators, and adjust the visibility of post-add call-to-action elements. No data is persisted beyond the session other than what is encoded in the URL and handled by backend interfaces.

components.functionality: On initial page load, reads couponId (and optional serviceId, servicePackageId, quantity) from the URL and calls getCouponDetail(couponId). Stores the returned coupon, applicable_services, and terms in page state for use by other components. Determines basic validity flags (active/inactive, expired/not expired) based on coupon.is_active and valid_from/valid_to.

Displays the coupon’s high-level details such as name, code (if provided), discount_type, discount_value, min_purchase_amount, max_discount_amount, and validity dates. If the coupon is inactive or outside its valid date range, displays a non-interactive notice and exposes flags for other components to disable actions.
Shows the list of terms and conditions for the coupon as returned by getCouponDetail.terms. Each term is output as a separate list item, with no hardcoded text.

Displays all applicable services for the coupon using getCouponDetail.applicable_services. Allows users to choose a specific service to filter eligible packages. When a service is selected, updates the URL query parameter serviceId and triggers a refresh of eligible packages. If the URL already contains a serviceId, preselects the matching service where available.

Fetches eligible service packages for the currently selected coupon (and optional selected service) by calling getEligibleServicePackagesForCoupon(couponId, serviceId). It runs after coupon-detail-data-loader has loaded the coupon and whenever serviceId in the URL changes. The component stores the list of eligible packages and makes it available to the list and selection components.

Displays the eligible service packages returned by eligible-service-packages-loader. For each package, shows the service_name, service_package.name, optional description, before_discount_price, estimated_discount_amount, and estimated_price_after_discount. Allows the user to select exactly one package for application of the coupon. When a package is selected, saves its ID in component state and updates the URL’s servicePackageId parameter.

Exposes the currently selected service package (from eligible-service-packages-list) together with the coupon and quantity fields. When submitted, calls addServiceWithCouponToCart(servicePackageId, couponId, quantity). Handles disabled states when no package is selected or the coupon is invalid. On success, passes the API response to the result banner and reveals cart navigation actions.

Listens for the result of addServiceWithCouponToCart. When success = true, shows a confirmation message using addServiceWithCouponToCart.message and key fields from added_item (display_name, quantity, unit_price, discount_amount, total_price). If success = false, displays the error message. Controls visibility of post-add actions like links to cart_checkout.html. Does not make additional API calls.

Uses the coupon’s category field from getCouponDetail.coupon to generate a link to services.html with the category query parameter set (e.g., services.html?category={coupon.category}). The same component also exposes a link/button to cart_checkout.html that becomes prominent after a successful add-to-cart operation.

Centralizes reading and updating of URL query parameters used by the page: couponId, serviceId, servicePackageId, and quantity. On initialization, parses the current URL and exposes these values to other components. When other components change selected service, package, or quantity, this component updates the URL accordingly without reloading the page (or triggers a controlled reload, depending on the app architecture). It ensures that state changes driven by user interactions are reflected in the URL rather than stored in localStorage.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column centered grid with a max-width content container. The two-column content section appears roughly 6/6 or 7/5 split between text and image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the linear, top-to-bottom structure from the mockup: global yellow utility bar and white header at the very top, followed by a full-width hero with a workshop/building photo and a bold left-aligned title (e.g., “Coupon Details”). Immediately below the hero, within the main content container, an overlapping two‑column section holds the coupon-specific content. The left column (roughly half the content width) carries the coupon-summary-panel at the top with the coupon-terms-list beneath it. The right column (the other half) carries the applicable-services-selector at the top, then the eligible-service-packages-list, with the add-to-cart-with-coupon-form aligned to the bottom/right of this column. Horizontal coupon-category-navigation links sit just under the hero title, spanning the content width. Status and loading components (add-to-cart-result-banner, data loaders, url-state-sync) are layered within or above these sections without breaking the overall layout flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the linear, top-to-bottom structure from the mockup: global yellow utility bar and white header at the very top, followed by a full-width hero with a workshop/building photo and a bold left-aligned title (e.g., “Coupon Details”). Immediately below the hero, within the main content container, an overlapping two‑column section holds the coupon-specific content. The left column (roughly half the content width) carries the coupon-summary-panel at the top with the coupon-terms-list beneath it. The right column (the other half) carries the applicable-services-selector at the top, then the eligible-service-packages-list, with the add-to-cart-with-coupon-form aligned to the bottom/right of this column. Horizontal coupon-category-navigation links sit just under the hero title, spanning the content width. Status and loading components (add-to-cart-result-banner, data loaders, url-state-sync) are layered within or above these sections without breaking the overall layout flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: As in the mockup, the header and utility bar together use the top ~10–15% of the viewport height. The hero image occupies about 25–35% of the initial viewport height, full-width, with the large white page title anchored on the left. The main coupon section (summary + services) fills the remaining first-screen height, using a 12-column centered grid. On large screens, the left column occupies about 5–6 of 12 columns, and the right column 6–7 of 12. The coupon-summary-panel slightly overlaps the bottom of the hero (using a negative top margin), with 16–24px padding inside and 32–48px spacing between major vertical blocks. Horizontal content margins sit around 80–120px from the viewport edges at large breakpoints, tightening on smaller screens. On narrow screens, the layout collapses to a single column with generous vertical spacing between sections.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains moderate, mirroring the brochure-like feel of the mockup. The coupon-summary-panel presents key details in a compact but readable block with comfortable line-height and 16–24px internal padding. The coupon-terms-list is paragraph-based or bullet-based text with clear spacing between items. The eligible-service-packages-list shows a small number of cards or rows per viewport height, each with enough whitespace to scan prices and discount details easily—no dense tables. Controls in the applicable-services-selector and add-to-cart-with-coupon-form are grouped with 8–16px gaps, avoiding visual clutter while remaining efficient.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow echoes the F-pattern from the mockup. The eye begins at the top-left (logo and header), moves across the top navigation, then down to the left-aligned hero title. Just below, the coupon-category-navigation provides a horizontal scan path. The overlapping coupon-summary-panel anchors attention in the upper-left of the main content. From there, the eye travels downward through the summary and terms before shifting right to the applicable-services-selector and eligible-service-packages-list. The add-to-cart-with-coupon-form is placed at the lower-right of the services column, following the natural reading path and reinforcing the primary action at the end of the F-pattern. The add-to-cart-result-banner, when visible, briefly takes precedence by appearing above the overlapping section, but it still respects the left-to-right reading direction.

layout.overall_description: Below the familiar yellow utility bar and white header, a full-width hero image of the workshop or building signage stretches across the screen, consuming roughly the top third of the viewport. On the left side of this hero, a large white heading such as “Coupon Details” sits over the image, aligned to the center-left, echoing the original ‘Services Provided’ styling. Just under this title, but still visually attached to the hero, a row of coupon-category-navigation links runs horizontally within the content width, giving users quick access to other offer categories. The main coupon area overlaps the bottom of the hero: a light, semi-transparent coupon-summary-panel floats in from the left, occupying about half the content width and partially sitting atop the workshop background. Directly beneath this summary on the left, the coupon terms flow downward in a clean text block. On the right, aligned to the same top baseline as the summary, the applicable-services-selector appears as a compact control bar above a tall vertical list of eligible service packages, which visually replaces the mechanic photo from the mockup as the main right-hand content. At the lower-right of this column, the add-to-cart-with-coupon form anchors the primary action, collecting the user’s final selections. If present, an add-to-cart-result-banner spans the content width just below the hero and above the overlapping panels, briefly drawing attention without disrupting the two-column structure. Throughout, generous side margins, 8px-based spacing, and clear typographic hierarchy ensure a practical, trustworthy small-business feel that closely mirrors the original design DNA.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-detail-data-loader is non-visual in its resting state but manifests as a centered loading indicator within the main content container when active. It occupies the same horizontal bounds as the primary content (the 12-column centered grid, roughly 80–120px inset from the viewport edges on large screens) and is vertically positioned in the upper part of the main coupon section, just below the hero and coupon-category-navigation. When loading, it can optionally dim or overlay the overlapping two-column region, ensuring the spinner or progress indicator appears centrally over where the coupon-summary-panel and eligible-service-packages-list will render, without extending into the header or hero areas.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as an overlay within the main content area: when active, it sits above the two-column coupon content with a semi-transparent backdrop, centered both horizontally and vertically within that section; when inactive, it does not reserve space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-summary-panel is the left-hand anchor of the overlapping section. It is positioned directly below the hero, with a slight negative top margin so that its top edge overlaps the lower portion of the hero background by approximately 24–32px, creating the same layered effect seen with the 'Logbook Servicing' panel in the mockup. On large screens, it spans about 5–6 of the 12 grid columns starting from the left edge of the content container and uses a light, semi-transparent white or light gray background so some of the underlying workshop imagery subtly shows through. The panel includes 16–24px padding on all sides and a modest drop shadow or subtle border to distinguish it from the hero. Vertically, it sits in the upper half of the main content area, with the coupon title and headline details near the top and the footer of the panel ending roughly halfway down the viewport when first loaded. On smaller screens, the panel becomes full-width, sitting entirely below the hero with 16–24px margin above and below, losing the overlap to maintain readability.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the page flow but uses a negative top margin to overlap the hero section slightly on larger screens, aligning to the grid and reflowing to a non-overlapping full-width block on smaller breakpoints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coupon-terms-list is positioned directly beneath the coupon-summary-panel and aligned to the same left column. It shares the same left and right grid boundaries as the summary (5–6 columns from the left at large breakpoints) and sits with a 16–24px vertical gap separating it from the bottom of the summary panel. This creates a continuous left-hand text column where the user naturally reads downward from key details into the finer print. The terms are presented as paragraphs or short bullet lists with 8–16px spacing between items, maintaining the moderate density of the mockup. The top of the terms list aligns roughly with the middle of the eligible-service-packages-list on the right, so the left column appears text-heavy but balanced by the taller list on the right. On mobile, the terms list follows the summary in a single column, full-width, with 16–24px vertical margin separating sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element that flows beneath the coupon-summary-panel within the left column, expanding vertically as needed without overlapping other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The applicable-services-selector sits at the top of the right-hand column, horizontally aligned with the top of the coupon-summary-panel so that both columns start on the same visual row. It occupies about 6–7 of the 12 grid columns on the right side of the content area, matching the width of the eligible-service-packages-list beneath it. The selector appears as a compact horizontal control bar (e.g., dropdowns, filter pills, or tabs) with 8–16px padding around its contents and 8–12px gaps between individual controls. A 16–24px vertical gap separates this selector from the eligible-service-packages-list below, creating a clear hierarchy: choose filtering options first, then view the list. On smaller screens, the selector spans full-width and appears immediately under the coupon-summary-panel (and possibly above the terms list if prioritization is desired), followed by the packages list, preserving the logical flow of selecting before scanning packages.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, pinned to the top of that column. On mobile layouts, it reflows to a full-width block placed above the eligible-service-packages-list, maintaining sectional hierarchy.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligible-service-packages-loader is visually scoped to the right column where service packages are displayed. When active, it overlays the area reserved for the eligible-service-packages-list, centered horizontally within the right 6–7 columns and vertically within the visible portion of that column. It may display a spinner or progress bar, with a light translucent mist over the packages area while data loads. It does not extend into the coupon-summary or terms on the left, maintaining the clean left-right separation. On smaller screens, the loader centers within the full-width block where the packages list will appear, positioned just below the applicable-services-selector.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlay within the right-hand content region; when loading, it sits above the eligible-service-packages-list area and below the applicable-services-selector, without altering the layout of adjacent sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligible-service-packages-list occupies the majority of the right-hand column and visually replaces the mechanic photo from the original mockup as the main content block on that side. It sits directly below the applicable-services-selector with a 16–24px vertical gap and spans about 6–7 of the 12 grid columns on large screens, aligning with the right edge of the content container. The list is vertically oriented, displaying each service package as a card or row with 16–24px vertical spacing between items and 16px internal padding per card. The top of this list aligns roughly with the mid-upper portion of the coupon-summary-panel on the left, and the bottom often extends slightly lower than the left column (depending on number of packages), which subtly pulls the eye down the right side after reading the text. On smaller screens, the list becomes full-width and appears beneath the applicable-services-selector and, depending on the chosen stacking order, either beneath or just after the coupon-terms-list, ensuring continuity in the reading flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, expanding vertically as needed. It respects the main content grid and does not overlap other components; it simply pushes the add-to-cart-with-coupon-form downward if the list is long.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The add-to-cart-with-coupon-form is positioned at the lower portion of the right column, directly after the eligible-service-packages-list.
